basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Isrāʾ — “The Night Journey” — opens with a radiant glorification: “Glory to Him who carried His servant by night from the Sacred Mosque to the Farthest Mosque, whose surroundings We have blessed, to show him of Our signs.” Qushayrī makes of the isrāʾ the journey of nearness: not the one who comes of himself, but the one whom his Lord transports — one bearer, the other borne; one willing, the other willed. The sūra then unrolls the story of the Children of Israel and their two corruptions, and proclaims that this Qurʾān “guides to that which is most upright.” It sets out the table of duties: the worship of the one God, filial devotion — “say not to them ‘uff’ nor rebuke them, but speak to them a generous word, and lower to them the wing of humility out of mercy” —, the just measure, the sanctity of life. It confers dignity upon the children of Adam: “We have honored the children of Adam,” an honor Qushayrī calls gratuitous, no counterpart of any act. It sings the universal tasbīḥ: “the seven heavens and the earth and all within them glorify Him; there is nothing that does not glorify His praise, though you understand not their glorification” — every particle of existence is a proof of the Lordship. It touches the mystery of the Spirit, “of the command of my Lord,” and seals the Qurʾān as “a healing and a mercy for the believers,” before the final praise of pure transcendence.
themes: the Children of Israel and their two corruptions
the Qurʾān that guides to the most upright
the duties: oneness, filial devotion, just measure
the dignity of the children of Adam

versets.ar: سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي أَسْرَىٰ بِعَبْدِهِ لَيْلًا مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ الْأَقْصَى الَّذِي بَارَكْنَا حَوْلَهُ لِنُرِيَهُ مِنْ آيَاتِنَا ۚ إِنَّهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْبَصِيرُ
versets.tr: subḥāna lladhī asrā bi-ʿabdihi laylan mina l-masjidi l-ḥarāmi ilā l-masjidi l-aqṣā lladhī bāraknā ḥawlahu li-nuriyahu min āyātinā innahu huwa s-samīʿu l-baṣīru
versets.trad: Glory to Him who carried His servant by night from the Sacred Mosque to the Farthest Mosque, whose surroundings We have blessed, to show him of Our signs. Truly He is the All-Hearing, the All-Seeing.
versets.commentaire: The sūra opens with the praise God offers to Himself: He has glorified Himself by His own Word and proclaimed His transcendence. Qushayrī notes that by ascribing to His Prophet the verb asrā (“He carried by night”), God removes in advance all astonishment and all vanity: this one does not come of himself as comes one whom his Lord transports; one is bearer, the other borne; one in separation, the other in union; one willing, the other willed. The word bi-ʿabdihi, “His servant,” is the summit of honor: the night was chosen “when the watchers sleep,” with no appointment and no preparation, for the night of union is purer than months and years. And “to show him of Our signs”: it was first through the signs, then through the attributes, then the unveiling by the Essence.

mots.def: nocturnal journey; the subject is God, the Prophet is borne.
supreme title of honor: pure servanthood.

mots.racine: س ج د / ق ص و

versets.ar: وَآتَيْنَا مُوسَى الْكِتَابَ وَجَعَلْنَاهُ هُدًى لِبَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ أَلَّا تَتَّخِذُوا مِنْ دُونِي وَكِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-ātaynā mūsā l-kitāba wa-jaʿalnāhu hudan li-banī isrāʾīla allā tattakhidhū min dūnī wakīlan
versets.trad: And We gave Moses the Book and made it a guidance for the Children of Israel, enjoining them: take no guardian besides Me.
versets.commentaire: Moses was sent with the Book as was our Prophet. Yet Qushayrī notes a difference in hearing: our Prophet was more perfect in reception, for the sun, at its rising and shining, is nearer to the one upon whom it rises than to its own realities. The Book is “guidance,” and guidance is to take none but God as the soul's support and trustee.

mots.def: the gift of the Book to Moses.
one to whom one entrusts one's affairs.

versets.ar: ذُرِّيَّةَ مَنْ حَمَلْنَا مَعَ نُوحٍ ۚ إِنَّهُ كَانَ عَبْدًا شَكُورًا
versets.tr: dhurriyyata man ḥamalnā maʿa nūḥin innahu kāna ʿabdan shakūran
versets.trad: O descendants of those We carried [in the Ark] with Noah. He was a grateful servant.
versets.commentaire: “O seed of those We carried with Noah”: Noah was “a deeply grateful servant.” Qushayrī unfolds gratitude: the grateful one is he whose thanksgiving itself rests upon the grace God gave him of being grateful, and who never falls short of the praise due to His blessings. He adds: the grateful one thanks with his wealth by spending it in God's way without hoarding, with his person by employing it in obedience, and with his heart, no hour of which passes without his remembering his Lord.

versets.ar: وَقَضَيْنَا إِلَىٰ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ فِي الْكِتَابِ لَتُفْسِدُنَّ فِي الْأَرْضِ مَرَّتَيْنِ وَلَتَعْلُنَّ عُلُوًّا كَبِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-qaḍaynā ilā banī isrāʾīla fi l-kitābi la-tufsidunna fi l-arḍi marratayni wa-la-taʿlunna ʿuluwwan kabīran
versets.trad: We decreed in the Book, addressing the Children of Israel: “You will surely spread corruption on the earth twice over, and you will rise up in great arrogance.”
versets.commentaire: The “decree” here means the announcement: God informs them of what will come from them. Qushayrī: warning them of the future increases their certainty when they meet what was foretold, makes the proof more binding against them, and makes them grasp that what the decree has preceded will inevitably come about, even if they reckon its term far off.

mots.def: qaḍāʾ here in the sense of prior notice.
emphatic with lām and heavy nūn.

mots.racine: ع ل و / ك ب ر

versets.ar: فَإِذَا جَاءَ وَعْدُ أُولَاهُمَا بَعَثْنَا عَلَيْكُمْ عِبَادًا لَنَا أُولِي بَأْسٍ شَدِيدٍ فَجَاسُوا خِلَالَ الدِّيَارِ ۚ وَكَانَ وَعْدًا مَفْعُولًا
versets.tr: fa-idhā jāʾa waʿdu ūlāhumā baʿathnā ʿalaykum ʿibādan lanā ulī baʾsin shadīdin fa-jāsū khilāla d-diyāri wa-kāna waʿdan mafʿūlan
versets.trad: When the time of the first of these came to pass, We sent against you servants of Ours of terrible might. They penetrated the dwellings, and the promise was fulfilled.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from this verse a law of Providence: God prepares peoples for particular conditions, so that when the time of His will concerning them arrives, those very ones are present and ready. The instruments of punishment were raised up beforehand; nothing comes to pass outside the decree.

mots.racine: و ع د / أ و ل
mots.sens: the time of the first of the two

mots.racine: ب أ س / ش د د

versets.ar: ثُمَّ رَدَدْنَا لَكُمُ الْكَرَّةَ عَلَيْهِمْ وَأَمْدَدْنَاكُمْ بِأَمْوَالٍ وَبَنِينَ وَجَعَلْنَاكُمْ أَكْثَرَ نَفِيرًا
versets.tr: thumma radadnā lakumu l-karrata ʿalayhim wa-amdadnākum bi-amwālin wa-banīna wa-jaʿalnākum akthara nafīran
versets.trad: Then We gave you back the turn against them, We reinforced you with wealth and sons, and We made you greater in number.
versets.commentaire: This verse, says Qushayrī, shows that God is the One who decrees the deeds of servants and orders their undertakings: even their victory over their enemies is part of what they acquire, and yet God declares that He Himself is its author — “We gave you back the turn against them.” The deed is theirs by acquisition, God's by creation.

versets.ar: إِنْ أَحْسَنْتُمْ أَحْسَنْتُمْ لِأَنْفُسِكُمْ ۖ وَإِنْ أَسَأْتُمْ فَلَهَا ۚ فَإِذَا جَاءَ وَعْدُ الْآخِرَةِ لِيَسُوءُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ وَلِيَدْخُلُوا الْمَسْجِدَ كَمَا دَخَلُوهُ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ وَلِيُتَبِّرُوا مَا عَلَوْا تَتْبِيرًا
versets.tr: in aḥsantum aḥsantum li-anfusikum wa-in asaʾtum fa-lahā fa-idhā jāʾa waʿdu l-ākhirati li-yasūʾū wujūhakum wa-li-yadkhulū l-masjida kamā dakhalūhu awwala marratin wa-li-yutabbirū mā ʿalaw tatbīran
versets.trad: If you do good, you do it for yourselves; and if you do evil, it is against yourselves. When the time of the second came, it was to bring affliction upon you, that your enemies might enter the temple as they entered it the first time, and utterly destroy all they overcame.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses the justice: if you do good, you have earned its reward; if you do evil, you have drawn its enmity upon yourselves — and God is too glorious for any adornment to accrue to Him from the acts of His servants, or any blemish to reach Him. Good and evil alike return to their doer; the Real remains above all gain and all harm.

mots.def: to do the beautiful and the good.

versets.ar: عَسَىٰ رَبُّكُمْ أَنْ يَرْحَمَكُمْ ۚ وَإِنْ عُدْتُمْ عُدْنَا ۘ وَجَعَلْنَا جَهَنَّمَ لِلْكَافِرِينَ حَصِيرًا
versets.tr: ʿasā rabbukum an yarḥamakum wa-in ʿudtum ʿudnā wa-jaʿalnā jahannama li-l-kāfirīna ḥaṣīran
versets.trad: It may be that your Lord will have mercy upon you. But if you return, We shall return. And We have made Hell a prison for the disbelievers.
versets.commentaire: The word ʿasā (“it may be”) carries hope and invitation: God set them upon the very edge of hope and expectation, of fear and trembling. Qushayrī modulates the “if you return”: if you return to sin, We return to punishment; but if you return to purity, We return to fidelity; if you return to seek refuge, We return to grant it; if you return to what befits you, We return to what befits Our generosity. And the believer, though a sinner, who leaves this world upon his faith, will surely one day reach forgiveness.

versets.ar: إِنَّ هَٰذَا الْقُرْآنَ يَهْدِي لِلَّتِي هِيَ أَقْوَمُ وَيُبَشِّرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ الَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ الصَّالِحَاتِ أَنَّ لَهُمْ أَجْرًا كَبِيرًا
versets.tr: inna hādhā l-qurʾāna yahdī li-llatī hiya aqwamu wa-yubashshiru l-muʾminīna lladhīna yaʿmalūna ṣ-ṣāliḥāti anna lahum ajran kabīran
versets.trad: Truly this Qurʾān guides to that which is most upright, and gives good tidings to the believers who do righteous deeds that theirs is a great reward,
versets.commentaire: The Qurʾān points to the true and the right. Qushayrī: the Qurʾān is a manifest proof; if anyone strays, the fault lies with the seeker, not with the proof: the proof may be radiant while the seeker turns away and neglects the manners of true vision. And again: the Qurʾān is a light; whoever is illumined by it escapes the darkness of his ignorance and emerges from the flood of his doubts; but he whose eyes of insight are inflamed has his guidance confounded.

versets.ar: وَأَنَّ الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ أَعْتَدْنَا لَهُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-anna lladhīna lā yuʾminūna bi-l-ākhirati aʿtadnā lahum ʿadhāban alīman
versets.trad: and that for those who do not believe in the Hereafter We have prepared a painful punishment.
versets.commentaire: The other face of the good tidings: for whoever refuses to believe in the Hereafter, a painful punishment is held ready. Qushayrī teaches elsewhere that the Qurʾān is mercy for some and trial for others: one and the same speech, but light for the heart that opens and crushing weight for the one that closes. Denial of the Hereafter is the root of this veil.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / أ ل م

versets.ar: وَيَدْعُ الْإِنْسَانُ بِالشَّرِّ دُعَاءَهُ بِالْخَيْرِ ۖ وَكَانَ الْإِنْسَانُ عَجُولًا
versets.tr: wa-yadʿu l-insānu bi-sh-sharri duʿāʾahu bi-l-khayri wa-kāna l-insānu ʿajūlan
versets.trad: Man calls for evil as he calls for good. Man is ever hasty.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a school of supplication: the proper manner is that the servant ask only at the moment of need, then look: if the matter does not concern him, let him refrain from it, according to the hadith “part of the beauty of a man's islam is that he leaves what does not concern him.” And when the answer is delayed, let him not accuse the Real, but know that the good may lie in the refusal; haste is not praised, and the best is repose and contentment with the decree, for God's choice for the servant is better than the servant's choice for himself.

mots.def: man sometimes presses for what harms him.

versets.ar: وَجَعَلْنَا اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهَارَ آيَتَيْنِ ۖ فَمَحَوْنَا آيَةَ اللَّيْلِ وَجَعَلْنَا آيَةَ النَّهَارِ مُبْصِرَةً لِتَبْتَغُوا فَضْلًا مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ وَلِتَعْلَمُوا عَدَدَ السِّنِينَ وَالْحِسَابَ ۚ وَكُلَّ شَيْءٍ فَصَّلْنَاهُ تَفْصِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-jaʿalnā l-layla wa-n-nahāra āyatayni fa-maḥawnā āyata l-layli wa-jaʿalnā āyata n-nahāri mubṣiratan li-tabtaghū faḍlan min rabbikum wa-li-taʿlamū ʿadada s-sinīna wa-l-ḥisāba wa-kulla shayʾin faṣṣalnāhu tafṣīlan
versets.trad: We have made the night and the day two signs. We made the sign of the night dark and the sign of the day giving sight, that you might seek bounty from your Lord and know the number of the years and the reckoning. And everything We have set out in detail.
versets.commentaire: Night and day are signs of God's perfect power and unicity, in their alternation, their waxing and waning. Qushayrī adds: He made of them a fitting time for worship and for steadfastness in knowing His majesty; worship has continuity as its condition, and if a lapse slips in, it is made up by compensation. He also sees the contrast of moon and sun: the moon does not stay two nights in one state, while the sun persists — so too with people: the masters of stability have permanence as their condition, and the people of fluctuation have change as their lot.

mots.def: to remove brightness from the night.

mots.sens: We set it out in detail

versets.ar: وَكُلَّ إِنْسَانٍ أَلْزَمْنَاهُ طَائِرَهُ فِي عُنُقِهِ ۖ وَنُخْرِجُ لَهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ كِتَابًا يَلْقَاهُ مَنْشُورًا
versets.tr: wa-kulla insānin alzamnāhu ṭāʾirahu fī ʿunuqihi wa-nukhriju lahu yawma l-qiyāmati kitāban yalqāhu manshūran
versets.trad: Every man's deed We have fastened to his neck, and on the Day of Resurrection We shall bring forth for him a book that he will find wide open:
versets.commentaire: Each man bears fastened to his neck what he has knotted of his deeds. Qushayrī images the two destinies: for the people of felicity He has saddled the mount of divine support, which carries them to the courtyards of salvation; for the people of misery He has mounted them upon the steed of abandonment, which holds them back from rushing toward the path of deliverance, and they fall into the pit of ruin.

mots.def: metaphor for the destiny one forges.

versets.ar: اقْرَأْ كِتَابَكَ كَفَىٰ بِنَفْسِكَ الْيَوْمَ عَلَيْكَ حَسِيبًا
versets.tr: iqraʾ kitābaka kafā bi-nafsika l-yawma ʿalayka ḥasīban
versets.trad: “Read your book! This day your own self suffices as a reckoner against you.”
versets.commentaire: “Read your book”: whoever was assisted by eternal solicitude finds himself preserved, in his dealings, from what would bring his ruin on the day of reckoning. Qushayrī reports a piercing supplication: he who is judged by his book finds that book clinging to him; then he says: Lord, do not judge me by my book, but by Your word — You are the Forgiver of sin and the Accepter of repentance; do not deal with me according to my register, for it holds my undoing and my ruin.

versets.ar: مَنِ اهْتَدَىٰ فَإِنَّمَا يَهْتَدِي لِنَفْسِهِ ۖ وَمَنْ ضَلَّ فَإِنَّمَا يَضِلُّ عَلَيْهَا ۚ وَلَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَىٰ ۗ وَمَا كُنَّا مُعَذِّبِينَ حَتَّىٰ نَبْعَثَ رَسُولًا
versets.tr: mani htadā fa-innamā yahtadī li-nafsihi wa-man ḍalla fa-innamā yaḍillu ʿalayhā wa-lā taziru wāziratun wizra ukhrā wa-mā kunnā muʿadhdhibīna ḥattā nabʿatha rasūlan
versets.trad: Whoever is guided is guided only for himself, and whoever strays strays only against himself. No bearer of burdens bears the burden of another. Never have We punished until We had sent a messenger.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the servant's deeds are confined to himself: if they are obedience, their light is for their doers; if they are lapse, their trial falls upon them. The Real is rich, holy, one, transcendent. And “We do not punish until We raise up a messenger” shows, he says, that obligations are imposed only from the moment of revealed announcement — responsibility is born of the warning.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا أَرَدْنَا أَنْ نُهْلِكَ قَرْيَةً أَمَرْنَا مُتْرَفِيهَا فَفَسَقُوا فِيهَا فَحَقَّ عَلَيْهَا الْقَوْلُ فَدَمَّرْنَاهَا تَدْمِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-idhā aradnā an nuhlika qaryatan amarnā mutrafīhā fa-fasaqū fīhā fa-ḥaqqa ʿalayhā l-qawlu fa-dammarnāhā tadmīran
versets.trad: When We willed to destroy a town, We first commanded its affluent ones [to obey], but they committed wickedness therein. So the word against it came due, and We destroyed it utterly.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī describes the rise of corruption: when the people of depravity multiply and prevail, and the people of uprightness grow few and vanish, then God submerges creation in His trial; no protector remains to intercede, no soul implores and is heard; He takes back His friends, leaves the corrupters, and the trial intensifies until God casts upon mankind the gaze of mercy.

versets.ar: وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا مِنَ الْقُرُونِ مِنْ بَعْدِ نُوحٍ ۗ وَكَفَىٰ بِرَبِّكَ بِذُنُوبِ عِبَادِهِ خَبِيرًا بَصِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-kam ahlaknā mina l-qurūni min baʿdi nūḥin wa-kafā bi-rabbika bi-dhunūbi ʿibādihi khabīran baṣīran
versets.trad: How many generations We have destroyed after Noah! Your Lord suffices as One Aware and Seeing of the sins of His servants.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī finds in this verse a consolation for the oppressed who grow impatient when the ruin of the oppressors is slow to come: let them ponder the past nations, their likes, how they built high and hoped far, then all perished together; they will know that the last will soon be threaded into the same row. And when the clouds of desolation overshadow them, let them take shelter in the shade of contemplating the decree: then desolation lifts, life turns sweet, and reverent awe takes hold.

mots.racine: خ ب ر / ب ص ر

versets.ar: مَنْ كَانَ يُرِيدُ الْعَاجِلَةَ عَجَّلْنَا لَهُ فِيهَا مَا نَشَاءُ لِمَنْ نُرِيدُ ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَا لَهُ جَهَنَّمَ يَصْلَاهَا مَذْمُومًا مَدْحُورًا
versets.tr: man kāna yurīdu l-ʿājilata ʿajjalnā lahu fīhā mā nashāʾu li-man nurīdu thumma jaʿalnā lahu jahannama yaṣlāhā madhmūman madḥūran
versets.trad: Whoever desires the fleeting life, We hasten for him therein what We will, for whom We will. Then We appoint for him Hell, which he enters, condemned and rejected.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever has been content with the base portion of this passing world remains deprived of the precious things of the Hereafter; he obtains only as much as he has inhaled of it, then, at the very moment his heart finds in it the most rest and quietude, he is torn from it — and nothing he had amassed of His bounties remains to him: he is kept from His nearness in the world to come. The fleeting chosen excludes the eternal offered.

mots.racine: ذ م م / د ح ر

versets.ar: وَمَنْ أَرَادَ الْآخِرَةَ وَسَعَىٰ لَهَا سَعْيَهَا وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَأُولَٰئِكَ كَانَ سَعْيُهُمْ مَشْكُورًا
versets.tr: wa-man arāda l-ākhirata wa-saʿā lahā saʿyahā wa-huwa muʾminun fa-ulāʾika kāna saʿyuhum mashkūran
versets.trad: But whoever desires the Hereafter and strives for it with due striving, being a believer — their striving shall be acknowledged.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the sign of one who truly wants the Hereafter is that he make for it the striving due to it; for the will of the Hereafter, stripped of works, is mere intention, and the striving is not accepted. And “being a believer”: believing too that his salvation is by God's grace, not by his own cause. The acknowledged striving is then multiplied: like alms, the servant's obedience God causes to grow and bear fruit.

mots.sens: he strove for it the due striving

versets.ar: كُلًّا نُمِدُّ هَٰؤُلَاءِ وَهَٰؤُلَاءِ مِنْ عَطَاءِ رَبِّكَ ۚ وَمَا كَانَ عَطَاءُ رَبِّكَ مَحْظُورًا
versets.tr: kullan numiddu hāʾulāʾi wa-hāʾulāʾi min ʿaṭāʾi rabbika wa-mā kāna ʿaṭāʾu rabbika maḥẓūran
versets.trad: To all — to these and to those — We extend the gift of your Lord. And the gift of your Lord is withheld from none.
versets.commentaire: God provides for each according to His power. Qushayrī distributes the portions: to one people salvation, to another the degrees; to one people safekeeping, to another honor; to one people His reward, to another His nearness. The Lord's gift, in this world, is forbidden to none — even one who desires only the fleeting receives his share.

versets.ar: انْظُرْ كَيْفَ فَضَّلْنَا بَعْضَهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ ۚ وَلَلْآخِرَةُ أَكْبَرُ دَرَجَاتٍ وَأَكْبَرُ تَفْضِيلًا
versets.tr: unẓur kayfa faḍḍalnā baʿḍahum ʿalā baʿḍin wa-la-l-ākhiratu akbaru darajātin wa-akbaru tafḍīlan
versets.trad: See how We have favored some over others. Yet the Hereafter is greater in degrees and greater in favor.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes the orders of preference: the worshippers are preferred one over another according to the purity of their works; the knowers, according to the limpidity of their states — purity of works by sincerity, limpidity of states by election. Some are distinguished by the truthfulness of the step, others by the loftiness of their aspiration. And in the Hereafter, he adds, the worshippers are distinguished by degrees, but the people of the Presence by their subtle graces, by the intimacy of the breath of nearness — which no exposition describes, no allusion grasps: one contemplates the Presence once a week, another never leaves it an instant.

versets.ar: لَا تَجْعَلْ مَعَ اللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا آخَرَ فَتَقْعُدَ مَذْمُومًا مَخْذُولًا
versets.tr: lā tajʿal maʿa llāhi ilāhan ākhara fa-taqʿuda madhmūman makhdhūlan
versets.trad: Set not up with God another deity, lest you sit condemned and forsaken.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses the fate of the associator: whoever has associated another with God finds himself blamed by God and forsaken by the very one he worshipped besides God — for no deity besides God can help its worshipper or turn harm from him. The idol abandons at the moment of need; God alone does not abandon.

mots.racine: أ ل ه / أ خ ر
ذ م م / خ ذ ل

versets.ar: وَقَضَىٰ رَبُّكَ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوا إِلَّا إِيَّاهُ وَبِالْوَالِدَيْنِ إِحْسَانًا ۚ إِمَّا يَبْلُغَنَّ عِنْدَكَ الْكِبَرَ أَحَدُهُمَا أَوْ كِلَاهُمَا فَلَا تَقُلْ لَهُمَا أُفٍّ وَلَا تَنْهَرْهُمَا وَقُلْ لَهُمَا قَوْلًا كَرِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-qaḍā rabbuka allā taʿbudū illā iyyāhu wa-bi-l-wālidayni iḥsānan immā yablughanna ʿindaka l-kibara aḥaduhumā aw kilāhumā fa-lā taqul lahumā uffin wa-lā tanharhumā wa-qul lahumā qawlan karīman
versets.trad: Your Lord has decreed that you worship none but Him, and that you be good to your parents. If one or both of them reach old age with you, say not to them “uff” nor rebuke them, but speak to them a generous word.
versets.commentaire: God commands that worship be reserved for Him through sincerity, the servant being overcome by the dominion of the Real until he is kept even from beholding his own worship. Then He prescribes goodness to parents: to stand at their service, attend to their honor, show no weariness at their bidding. Qushayrī draws a subtle thread: God commanded respect for the right of parents, who are of the same kind as the servant; now whoever is unable to fulfill the right of his own kind, how shall he fulfill the right of his Lord?

mots.racine: و ل د / ح س ن
ق و ل / ك ر م

versets.ar: وَاخْفِضْ لَهُمَا جَنَاحَ الذُّلِّ مِنَ الرَّحْمَةِ وَقُلْ رَبِّ ارْحَمْهُمَا كَمَا رَبَّيَانِي صَغِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-khfiḍ lahumā janāḥa dh-dhulli mina r-raḥmati wa-qul rabbi rḥamhumā kamā rabbayānī ṣaghīran
versets.trad: And lower to them the wing of humility out of mercy, and say: “My Lord, have mercy upon them as they reared me when I was small.”
versets.commentaire: “Lower to them the wing of humility”: Qushayrī unfolds it into concrete gestures — by gentleness of treatment and softness of speech, eagerness in service, promptness to answer, refusal to weary of their requests, patience with them, and that you withhold from them nothing within your power. The lowered wing is love made flesh.

mots.racine: ج ن ح / ذ ل ل

versets.ar: رَبُّكُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا فِي نُفُوسِكُمْ ۚ إِنْ تَكُونُوا صَالِحِينَ فَإِنَّهُ كَانَ لِلْأَوَّابِينَ غَفُورًا
versets.tr: rabbukum aʿlamu bimā fī nufūsikum in takūnū ṣāliḥīna fa-innahu kāna li-l-awwābīna ghafūran
versets.trad: Your Lord knows best what is in your souls. If you are righteous, then truly He is Forgiving to those who turn back to Him.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws the grace that sincerity of heart earns: when God knows the truthfulness of a servant's heart, He aids him with beautiful supports, honors him with unbroken assistance, makes the hard easy for him, guards him from evils, and inclines the hearts of the multitude toward him. God, who knows the depths of souls, is forgiving to the “returners” (awwābīn) who never cease turning back to Him.

mots.def: those who turn back to God again and again.

versets.ar: وَآتِ ذَا الْقُرْبَىٰ حَقَّهُ وَالْمِسْكِينَ وَابْنَ السَّبِيلِ وَلَا تُبَذِّرْ تَبْذِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-āti dha l-qurbā ḥaqqahu wa-l-miskīna wa-bna s-sabīli wa-lā tubadhdhir tabdhīran
versets.trad: Give the near kinsman his due, and the poor, and the wayfarer, but do not squander wastefully.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī broadens the meaning of “the due to be given”: the fulfillment of the due is by wealth, by the person, by word and by deed; whoever keeps to the measure of the due and gives all for what God demands of him, he it is who fulfills what the Real has imposed by His command. As for squandering (tabdhīr), it is to overstep the limit set by the command and the permission: what goes to the soul's portion — even a sesame seed — is wastefulness; what goes to Him — even the giving of the whole self — is still falling short.

mots.racine: ب ن ي / س ب ل

versets.ar: إِنَّ الْمُبَذِّرِينَ كَانُوا إِخْوَانَ الشَّيَاطِينِ ۖ وَكَانَ الشَّيْطَانُ لِرَبِّهِ كَفُورًا
versets.tr: inna l-mubadhdhirīna kānū ikhwāna sh-shayāṭīni wa-kāna sh-shayṭānu li-rabbihi kafūran
versets.trad: Truly the squanderers are the brothers of the devils, and the devil was ungrateful to his Lord.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī explains this brotherhood: they are the brothers of the devils because they spend according to their passion and follow, along its path, the suggestions and whisperings of the devils; and when this leads them into disobedience, they deserve to be called brothers of the devils. Squandering is no mere excess of spending: it is a disguised obedience to the spirit of ingratitude.

mots.racine: أ خ و / ش ط ن

versets.ar: وَإِمَّا تُعْرِضَنَّ عَنْهُمُ ابْتِغَاءَ رَحْمَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّكَ تَرْجُوهَا فَقُلْ لَهُمْ قَوْلًا مَيْسُورًا
versets.tr: wa-immā tuʿriḍanna ʿanhumu btighāʾa raḥmatin min rabbika tarjūhā fa-qul lahum qawlan maysūran
versets.trad: And if you must turn away from them, seeking a mercy from your Lord for which you hope, then speak to them a gentle word.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if your means do not allow the kindness they ask of you, dismiss them with a beautiful promise, when you cannot relieve them with a generous gift; for the promise of the noble is sweeter than the gift of the miserly. When the hand is empty, let the word stay full.

mots.def: to withdraw for lack of means to give.

mots.racine: ب غ ي / ر ح م
mots.def: the quest for a good from God.

mots.racine: ق و ل / ي س ر

versets.ar: وَلَا تَجْعَلْ يَدَكَ مَغْلُولَةً إِلَىٰ عُنُقِكَ وَلَا تَبْسُطْهَا كُلَّ الْبَسْطِ فَتَقْعُدَ مَلُومًا مَحْسُورًا
versets.tr: wa-lā tajʿal yadaka maghlūlatan ilā ʿunuqika wa-lā tabsuṭhā kulla l-basṭi fa-taqʿuda malūman maḥsūran
versets.trad: Make not your hand chained to your neck, nor open it wide altogether, lest you sit blamed and bereft.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sums up the middle way: do not hold back your hand to the point of becoming miserly, nor stretch it out in excess to the point of exhausting yourself in giving; hold between the two a middle path. Neither the avarice that closes nor the prodigality that ruins: equilibrium is itself a spiritual virtue.

mots.def: to open the hand in giving.

mots.racine: ل و م / ح س ر

versets.ar: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ وَيَقْدِرُ ۚ إِنَّهُ كَانَ بِعِبَادِهِ خَبِيرًا بَصِيرًا
versets.tr: inna rabbaka yabsuṭu r-rizqa li-man yashāʾu wa-yaqdiru innahu kāna bi-ʿibādihi khabīran baṣīran
versets.trad: Truly your Lord extends provision wide to whom He wills, and measures it. He is Aware and Seeing of His servants.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, who here intends the “provision of states” as much as of goods, condenses: when He extends wide, no want remains; when He restricts, He exhausts every power. Largesse and measure are equally in His hands, who knows and sees the secret of each servant.

mots.racine: ب س ط / ر ز ق
خ ب ر / ب ص ر

versets.ar: وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا أَوْلَادَكُمْ خَشْيَةَ إِمْلَاقٍ ۖ نَحْنُ نَرْزُقُهُمْ وَإِيَّاكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ قَتْلَهُمْ كَانَ خِطْئًا كَبِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-lā taqtulū awlādakum khashyata imlāqin naḥnu narzuquhum wa-iyyākum inna qatlahum kāna khiṭʾan kabīran
versets.trad: Do not kill your children for fear of poverty. We provide for them and for you. Their killing is a great sin.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī grounds the serenity of the One who provides: whoever knows that the Provider is God lightens from his heart the care of his family, however many; but whoever is unaware that their provisions were apportioned before creation strays into the mazes of his erroneous reckoning, falling into them with heart and body — yet nothing comes to pass save what the decree has preceded. To kill from fear of poverty is to doubt the Guarantor.

mots.racine: خ ش ي / م ل ق
خ ط أ / ك ب ر

versets.ar: وَلَا تَقْرَبُوا الزِّنَا ۖ إِنَّهُ كَانَ فَاحِشَةً وَسَاءَ سَبِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-lā taqrabū z-zinā innahu kāna fāḥishatan wa-sāʾa sabīlan
versets.trad: And do not approach fornication; it is an abomination and an evil way.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī explains the particular gravity of fornication: it outweighs the other abominations because it ruins the sanctity of God's right and tears the sanctity of the right of men; then through the confusion of lineages it brings about, and the corruption it sows among kin, wounding honor and inflaming wrath.

versets.ar: وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا النَّفْسَ الَّتِي حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ إِلَّا بِالْحَقِّ ۗ وَمَنْ قُتِلَ مَظْلُومًا فَقَدْ جَعَلْنَا لِوَلِيِّهِ سُلْطَانًا فَلَا يُسْرِفْ فِي الْقَتْلِ ۖ إِنَّهُ كَانَ مَنْصُورًا
versets.tr: wa-lā taqtulū n-nafsa llatī ḥarrama llāhu illā bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-man qutila maẓlūman fa-qad jaʿalnā li-waliyyihi sulṭānan fa-lā yusrif fi l-qatli innahu kāna manṣūran
versets.trad: Do not take the life that God has made sacred, save by right. Whoever is slain unjustly, We have given his heir authority [to seek redress]; but let him not exceed in slaying, for he is [already] aided [by the law].
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī widens the prohibition to the self as well: it is not permitted to kill another without right, nor for a man to kill his own soul without right; and as killing the soul by the blade or its like is forbidden, so too is aiming at its ruin. Whoever persists in disobeying his Lord works at his own destruction. And on the “authority” given to the heir, he slips an allusion: the help comes from God, and the one defended by the Real sees neither his lance broken nor his arrows go astray.

mots.racine: ن ف س / ح ر م
mots.sens: the life God has made sacred

mots.racine: و ل ي / س ل ط

versets.ar: وَلَا تَقْرَبُوا مَالَ الْيَتِيمِ إِلَّا بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ حَتَّىٰ يَبْلُغَ أَشُدَّهُ ۚ وَأَوْفُوا بِالْعَهْدِ ۖ إِنَّ الْعَهْدَ كَانَ مَسْئُولًا
versets.tr: wa-lā taqrabū māla l-yatīmi illā bi-llatī hiya aḥsanu ḥattā yablugha ashuddahu wa-awfū bi-l-ʿahdi inna l-ʿahda kāna masʾūlan
versets.trad: Do not approach the orphan's property save in the way that is best, until he reaches maturity. And fulfill the covenant, for the covenant will be questioned.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: because the orphan had no one to attend to him, God commanded the stranger, who has no bond with him, to take charge of his affair; the child sits in repose, and the guardian toils in hardship. And he adds, glancing at the disciple's relation to the master: God's command laid upon the guardian is more profitable to the child than the tenderness of his own parents in their lifetime.

mots.racine: م و ل / ي ت م

mots.def: the age of strength and discernment.

mots.racine: و ف ي / ع ه د

versets.ar: وَأَوْفُوا الْكَيْلَ إِذَا كِلْتُمْ وَزِنُوا بِالْقِسْطَاسِ الْمُسْتَقِيمِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ وَأَحْسَنُ تَأْوِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-awfū l-kayla idhā kiltum wa-zinū bi-l-qisṭāsi l-mustaqīmi dhālika khayrun wa-aḥsanu taʾwīlan
versets.trad: Give full measure when you measure, and weigh with the straight balance. That is better, and best in outcome.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of trade a parable of recompense: as you judge you will be judged, as you deal you will be dealt with, as you measure it will be measured to you, as you are it will be done to you; whoever gives full, full will be given to him; whoever betrays, he will be betrayed. The just balance of the marketplace mirrors the just balance of the Judgment.

mots.racine: و ف ي / ك ي ل
ق س ط / ق و م

versets.ar: وَلَا تَقْفُ مَا لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِ عِلْمٌ ۚ إِنَّ السَّمْعَ وَالْبَصَرَ وَالْفُؤَادَ كُلُّ أُولَٰئِكَ كَانَ عَنْهُ مَسْئُولًا
versets.tr: wa-lā taqfu mā laysa laka bihi ʿilmun inna s-samʿa wa-l-baṣara wa-l-fuʾāda kullu ulāʾika kāna ʿanhu masʾūlan
versets.trad: Do not pursue that of which you have no knowledge. Hearing, sight, and heart — of all these one will be questioned.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when probable conjectures overcome you and the Real does not raise you to certainty, do not force yourself to rule without proof; and if something of the time's affairs perplexes you, return to God — if a face of the indication gleams to your heart on the edge of ambiguity, entrust its knowledge to God and stop where it stops. As for hearing, sight, and heart, these are the trusts of the Real with the servant: whoever employs them in obediences returns the trust intact and earns praise; whoever defiles them with transgressions betrays the trust and calls down blame.

mots.racine: س م ع / ب ص ر / ف أ د
mots.def: faculties for which one is answerable.

versets.ar: وَلَا تَمْشِ فِي الْأَرْضِ مَرَحًا ۖ إِنَّكَ لَنْ تَخْرِقَ الْأَرْضَ وَلَنْ تَبْلُغَ الْجِبَالَ طُولًا
versets.tr: wa-lā tamshi fi l-arḍi maraḥan innaka lan takhriqa l-arḍa wa-lan tablugha l-jibāla ṭūlan
versets.trad: Do not walk upon the earth in arrogance. You will never tear the earth open, nor reach the mountains in height.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes pride a symptom of the heart's absence: haughtiness, tyranny, vanity and self-inflation — all this is the fruit of absence from the remembrance and of the veil that hides the contemplation of the Real; for when God manifests Himself to a thing, it humbles itself before Him. In the time of the heart's presence and the reign of remembrance, the heart is bowed, awe prevails, and every trace of vanity falls away. Whoever recalls that he was drawn from a drop and carries within him the residues of his food, his aspiration rises above belittling and pettiness.

mots.sens: you will not tear the earth

mots.racine: ب ل غ / ط و ل

versets.ar: كُلُّ ذَٰلِكَ كَانَ سَيِّئُهُ عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ مَكْرُوهًا
versets.tr: kullu dhālika kāna sayyiʾuhu ʿinda rabbika makrūhan
versets.trad: All of that — the evil of it is hateful in the sight of your Lord.
versets.commentaire: The evil of all this is hateful before God. Qushayrī shows the luminous reverse: when the steps come to know the felicity of frequenting the courtyards of contemplation, and the secrets are perfumed by the breath of nearness, the moments strip off the veil, the dominion of the Reality takes hold, and one is purified of these blamed attributes. The reproved evil dissolves in the Presence.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ مِمَّا أَوْحَىٰ إِلَيْكَ رَبُّكَ مِنَ الْحِكْمَةِ ۗ وَلَا تَجْعَلْ مَعَ اللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا آخَرَ فَتُلْقَىٰ فِي جَهَنَّمَ مَلُومًا مَدْحُورًا
versets.tr: dhālika mimmā awḥā ilayka rabbuka mina l-ḥikmati wa-lā tajʿal maʿa llāhi ilāhan ākhara fa-tulqā fī jahannama malūman madḥūran
versets.trad: That is part of the wisdom your Lord has revealed to you. Set not up with God another deity, lest you be cast into Hell, blamed and rejected.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two modes of transmission: God said to His Prophet: “That is part of the wisdom your Lord has revealed to you” — by revelation and announcement; and to His friends, it is by the decree of inspiration that He makes known. Wisdom descends to the Prophet by waḥy, to the saints by ilhām. Its seal is divine oneness, whose transgression casts one into Hell.

versets.ar: أَفَأَصْفَاكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ بِالْبَنِينَ وَاتَّخَذَ مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ إِنَاثًا ۚ إِنَّكُمْ لَتَقُولُونَ قَوْلًا عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: a-fa-aṣfākum rabbukum bi-l-banīna wa-ttakhadha mina l-malāʾikati ināthan innakum la-taqūlūna qawlan ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: Has your Lord then chosen sons for you and taken to Himself daughters from among the angels? You utter a monstrous word!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unmasks the incoherence of the associators: they deemed it possible that God should have a child, they reflected upon it, then were not content with that: they ascribed to Him what they disdained for themselves. They only added to their rebellion, their excess, and their distance from accepting the Real. Their word is “monstrous” because it imputes to the Most High what they themselves scorn.

mots.racine: ق و ل / ع ظ م

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ صَرَّفْنَا فِي هَٰذَا الْقُرْآنِ لِيَذَّكَّرُوا وَمَا يَزِيدُهُمْ إِلَّا نُفُورًا
versets.tr: wa-laqad ṣarrafnā fī hādhā l-qurʾāni li-yadhdhakkarū wa-mā yazīduhum illā nufūran
versets.trad: We have expounded things variously in this Qurʾān that they might take heed, yet it only increases their aversion.
versets.commentaire: God has varied the examples in the Qurʾān that they might remember, but the veil turns grace into repulsion. Qushayrī teaches elsewhere that the Qurʾān is mercy and light for one whose inner sight God has illumined with the light of oneness, and crushing weight for one whose inner sight He has covered with the veil of denial — one and the same reminder, two destinies.

mots.def: to vary the modes of exposition.

versets.ar: قُلْ لَوْ كَانَ مَعَهُ آلِهَةٌ كَمَا يَقُولُونَ إِذًا لَابْتَغَوْا إِلَىٰ ذِي الْعَرْشِ سَبِيلًا
versets.tr: qul law kāna maʿahu ālihatun kamā yaqūlūna idhan la-btaghaw ilā dhi l-ʿarshi sabīlan
versets.trad: Say: “Were there gods with Him, as they claim, then they would surely seek a way to the Lord of the Throne.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sets out the proof of oneness by absurdity: if the Maker were more than one, there would be between them contradiction and obstruction, and incapacity would be verified in their quality — yet incapacity is a mark of created beings. A plurality of gods would imply rivalry, hence weakness: incompatible with divinity.

versets.ar: سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَىٰ عَمَّا يَقُولُونَ عُلُوًّا كَبِيرًا
versets.tr: subḥānahu wa-taʿālā ʿammā yaqūlūna ʿuluwwan kabīran
versets.trad: Glory be to Him, exalted far above what they say, with a great exaltation!
versets.commentaire: God declares Himself transcendent above what they ascribe to Him. Qushayrī: He exalted Himself by purifying Himself of associate and supporter, of helper and equal. The glorification (tasbīḥ) is itself the answer to every word that would attach a peer to Him.

mots.racine: ع ل و / ك ب ر

versets.ar: تُسَبِّحُ لَهُ السَّمَاوَاتُ السَّبْعُ وَالْأَرْضُ وَمَنْ فِيهِنَّ ۚ وَإِنْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا يُسَبِّحُ بِحَمْدِهِ وَلَٰكِنْ لَا تَفْقَهُونَ تَسْبِيحَهُمْ ۗ إِنَّهُ كَانَ حَلِيمًا غَفُورًا
versets.tr: tusabbiḥu lahu s-samāwātu s-sabʿu wa-l-arḍu wa-man fīhinna wa-in min shayʾin illā yusabbiḥu bi-ḥamdihi wa-lākin lā tafqahūna tasbīḥahum innahu kāna ḥalīman ghafūran
versets.trad: The seven heavens and the earth and all within them glorify Him. There is nothing that does not glorify His praise, though you understand not their glorification. Truly He is Forbearing, Forgiving.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two kinds of tasbīḥ: the living of the heavens and the earth glorify Him with a glorification of speech, and the non-living glorify Him by way of proof and indication; there is not a particle of beings or traces that is not a proof of the Lordship. Every thing is a language that says God; and if men marvel to hear oneness proclaimed, it is by their ignorance and the difficulty of their perception that they deny. The whole of creation prays; only the deafness of the soul does not hear it.

mots.racine: س م و / س ب ع

versets.ar: وَإِذَا قَرَأْتَ الْقُرْآنَ جَعَلْنَا بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَ الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ حِجَابًا مَسْتُورًا
versets.tr: wa-idhā qaraʾta l-qurʾāna jaʿalnā baynaka wa-bayna lladhīna lā yuʾminūna bi-l-ākhirati ḥijāban mastūran
versets.trad: When you recite the Qurʾān, We place between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter a hidden veil.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads this “veil” as a divine protection more than a punishment: We have brought you into the shelter of Our keeping, raised about you the pavilions of Our safekeeping, and turned away from you, by Our gentleness, the erring hands. The hidden veil wraps the Prophet in an imperceptible protection.

versets.ar: وَجَعَلْنَا عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ أَكِنَّةً أَنْ يَفْقَهُوهُ وَفِي آذَانِهِمْ وَقْرًا ۚ وَإِذَا ذَكَرْتَ رَبَّكَ فِي الْقُرْآنِ وَحْدَهُ وَلَّوْا عَلَىٰ أَدْبَارِهِمْ نُفُورًا
versets.tr: wa-jaʿalnā ʿalā qulūbihim akinnatan an yafqahūhu wa-fī ādhānihim waqran wa-idhā dhakarta rabbaka fi l-qurʾāni waḥdahu wallaw ʿalā adbārihim nufūran
versets.trad: We have placed coverings over their hearts, lest they understand it, and deafness in their ears. And when you mention your Lord alone in the Qurʾān, they turn their backs in aversion.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, faithful to his doctrine that God is the creator of all things down to the acquired acts of men, affirms: He has declared that He is the creator of their straying, and the One who fixes in their hearts what has nested there of their utter waywardness. And when you mention your Lord alone, they would rather you mention their idols; God has sealed their hearts, and no speech pleases them but the one that resembles them.

mots.racine: و ل ي / د ب ر

versets.ar: نَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يَسْتَمِعُونَ بِهِ إِذْ يَسْتَمِعُونَ إِلَيْكَ وَإِذْ هُمْ نَجْوَىٰ إِذْ يَقُولُ الظَّالِمُونَ إِنْ تَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا رَجُلًا مَسْحُورًا
versets.tr: naḥnu aʿlamu bimā yastamiʿūna bihi idh yastamiʿūna ilayka wa-idh hum najwā idh yaqūlu ẓ-ẓālimūna in tattabiʿūna illā rajulan masḥūran
versets.trad: We know best the intent with which they listen when they listen to you, and when they confer in secret, when the wrongdoers say: “You follow but a bewitched man!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they concealed from the Messenger their true states and feigned agreement, but God exposed them, unveiled their secrets, laid bare their ugliness and tore their veils. And he draws a spiritual law from it: what the inmost self holds must inevitably appear to the people of insight, through what shows upon the faces.

versets.ar: انْظُرْ كَيْفَ ضَرَبُوا لَكَ الْأَمْثَالَ فَضَلُّوا فَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ سَبِيلًا
versets.tr: unẓur kayfa ḍarabū laka l-amthāla fa-ḍallū fa-lā yastaṭīʿūna sabīlan
versets.trad: See how they coin comparisons for you! They have gone astray and can find no way.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they blamed him for what is no defect in itself, saying “you follow but a bewitched man”; but what defect would there be in his being one of the human kind? And he adds: the Real has taken charge of his help; his distinction lies neither in a constitution, nor a form, nor a craft — his honor appeared through all to which God's eternal grace and mercy attached themselves.

mots.racine: ض ر ب / م ث ل

mots.def: a path of return to truth.

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا أَإِذَا كُنَّا عِظَامًا وَرُفَاتًا أَإِنَّا لَمَبْعُوثُونَ خَلْقًا جَدِيدًا
versets.tr: wa-qālū a-idhā kunnā ʿiẓāman wa-rufātan a-innā la-mabʿūthūna khalqan jadīdan
versets.trad: They say: “When we are bones and dust, shall we really be raised up in a new creation?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī refutes the denial by the logic of the first creation: they acknowledged that God created them, then denied His power to bring them back after their annihilation; yet just as it was possible for Him to bring them into being a first time when they were in nothingness, with neither trace nor vestige, but within reach of His power and the object of His will, so it belongs to the Master of power and will to bring them back into existence a second time — thus, when the eye of the heart is inflamed, its owner can no longer discern.

mots.racine: ع ظ م / ر ف ت
خ ل ق / ج د د

versets.ar: قُلْ كُونُوا حِجَارَةً أَوْ حَدِيدًا
versets.tr: qul kūnū ḥijāratan aw ḥadīdan
versets.trad: Say: “[Yes, you shall be,] though you be stone or iron,
versets.commentaire: “Be stone or iron”: nothing eludes God. Qushayrī: He declared that no object of His power resists Him, for He is qualified by an eternal power of universal scope: neither toil nor ease enters His quality; the first creation and the re-creation are alike to Him. The hardest matter sets no limit to the Creator.

versets.ar: أَوْ خَلْقًا مِمَّا يَكْبُرُ فِي صُدُورِكُمْ ۚ فَسَيَقُولُونَ مَنْ يُعِيدُنَا ۖ قُلِ الَّذِي فَطَرَكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ۚ فَسَيُنْغِضُونَ إِلَيْكَ رُءُوسَهُمْ وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هُوَ ۖ قُلْ عَسَىٰ أَنْ يَكُونَ قَرِيبًا
versets.tr: aw khalqan mimmā yakburu fī ṣudūrikum fa-sa-yaqūlūna man yuʿīdunā quli lladhī faṭarakum awwala marratin fa-sa-yunghiḍūna ilayka ruʾūsahum wa-yaqūlūna matā huwa qul ʿasā an yakūna qarīban
versets.trad: or any creation that seems great in your breasts.” They will say: “Who will bring us back?” Say: “He who originated you the first time!” Then they will shake their heads at you and say: “When will it be?” Say: “Perhaps it is near.”
versets.commentaire: “He who shaped you the first time” will bring you back: Qushayrī insists on the eternity of the power, whose pre-eternity prevents what comes to be from affecting it — for Him there is neither increased toil nor relief between creating and re-creating. The “perhaps near” leaves the term in mystery while inscribing it as certain.

mots.racine: ك ب ر / ص د ر
mots.sens: that seems great in your breasts

versets.ar: يَوْمَ يَدْعُوكُمْ فَتَسْتَجِيبُونَ بِحَمْدِهِ وَتَظُنُّونَ إِنْ لَبِثْتُمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا
versets.tr: yawma yadʿūkum fa-tastajībūna bi-ḥamdihi wa-taẓunnūna in labithtum illā qalīlan
versets.trad: The Day He calls you, you will answer with His praise, and you will think you tarried but a little.
versets.commentaire: “He will call you and you will answer with His praise”: Qushayrī notes a subtlety — ḥamd is synonymous with thanksgiving, and the servant gives thanks only for a blessing; the verse thus indicates that even in their graves they are within His blessing. Praise at the call attests a grace received even at the heart of death.

mots.racine: ج و ب / ح م د

versets.ar: وَقُلْ لِعِبَادِي يَقُولُوا الَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ ۚ إِنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ يَنْزَغُ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ كَانَ لِلْإِنْسَانِ عَدُوًّا مُبِينًا
versets.tr: wa-qul li-ʿibādī yaqūlū llatī hiya aḥsanu inna sh-shayṭāna yanzaghu baynahum inna sh-shayṭāna kāna li-l-insāni ʿaduwwan mubīnan
versets.trad: Tell My servants to say that which is best, for Satan sows discord among them; Satan is to man a manifest enemy.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī grades “the best word”: the best word is the lover's acknowledgment of the servanthood due to his Beloved; the best word of the sinners is the confession of fault, and the best word of the knowers is the confession of incapacity to know — according to the Prophet's word: “Glory to You, I cannot enumerate Your praises; You are as You have praised Yourself.”

versets.ar: رَبُّكُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِكُمْ ۖ إِنْ يَشَأْ يَرْحَمْكُمْ أَوْ إِنْ يَشَأْ يُعَذِّبْكُمْ ۚ وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ عَلَيْهِمْ وَكِيلًا
versets.tr: rabbukum aʿlamu bikum in yashaʾ yarḥamkum aw in yashaʾ yuʿadhdhibkum wa-mā arsalnāka ʿalayhim wakīlan
versets.trad: Your Lord knows you best. If He wills, He will have mercy on you, or if He wills, He will punish you. We have not sent you as a guardian over them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He closed to each the path of self-knowledge, that every heart might cling to its Lord; He left the ends uncertain for their masters. And He placed the mention of mercy before that of punishment, that hope might be strengthened thereby. The servant knows the outward of his state; God alone knows his state and his end — hence the practice of saying: “I am a believer, God willing.”

versets.ar: وَرَبُّكَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنْ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۗ وَلَقَدْ فَضَّلْنَا بَعْضَ النَّبِيِّينَ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ ۖ وَآتَيْنَا دَاوُودَ زَبُورًا
versets.tr: wa-rabbuka aʿlamu bi-man fi s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-laqad faḍḍalnā baʿḍa n-nabiyyīna ʿalā baʿḍin wa-ātaynā dāwūda zabūran
versets.trad: Your Lord knows best whoever is in the heavens and the earth. We have favored some prophets over others, and We gave David the Psalms.
versets.commentaire: God has favored some prophets over others in prophethood, rank, message and subtle gifts. Qushayrī brings the hierarchy to its summit in our Prophet: He made our Prophet the most excellent of them: they are like the stars and he is among them the full moon; they are like full moons and he is among them the sun; they are suns, and he is the sun of suns.

mots.racine: س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: whoever is in the heavens and the earth

mots.racine: ف ض ل / ن ب أ

versets.ar: قُلِ ادْعُوا الَّذِينَ زَعَمْتُمْ مِنْ دُونِهِ فَلَا يَمْلِكُونَ كَشْفَ الضُّرِّ عَنْكُمْ وَلَا تَحْوِيلًا
versets.tr: quli dʿū lladhīna zaʿamtum min dūnihi fa-lā yamlikūna kashfa ḍ-ḍurri ʿankum wa-lā taḥwīlan
versets.trad: Say: “Call upon those you claim as gods besides Him: they have no power to remove harm from you, nor to avert it.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: seek help, in the trials that await you, from the idols you worship besides God — until you hold it verified that to worship anything besides God profits you not, and to abandon it harms you not. The very experience of the idols' impotence is a pedagogy toward oneness.

mots.racine: ك ش ف / ض ر ر

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ يَبْتَغُونَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمُ الْوَسِيلَةَ أَيُّهُمْ أَقْرَبُ وَيَرْجُونَ رَحْمَتَهُ وَيَخَافُونَ عَذَابَهُ ۚ إِنَّ عَذَابَ رَبِّكَ كَانَ مَحْذُورًا
versets.tr: ulāʾika lladhīna yadʿūna yabtaghūna ilā rabbihimu l-wasīlata ayyuhum aqrabu wa-yarjūna raḥmatahu wa-yakhāfūna ʿadhābahu inna ʿadhāba rabbika kāna maḥdhūran
versets.trad: Those very ones whom they call upon seek the means to their Lord — vying as to who shall be nearest — and they hope for His mercy and fear His punishment. Truly the punishment of your Lord is to be dreaded.
versets.commentaire: Those whom the idolaters invoke — such as the Messiah, Ezra, the angels — themselves seek the means (wasīla) of drawing near to God: how then could they lift another's trial? Qushayrī strikes with his comparisons: the attachment of creature to creature is the attachment of one prisoner to another; when the destitute joins the destitute, their destitution doubles; when the blind leads the blind, both fall together into the well.

mots.sens: the means, the way of access
mots.def: what draws one near to God.

versets.ar: وَإِنْ مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ إِلَّا نَحْنُ مُهْلِكُوهَا قَبْلَ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ أَوْ مُعَذِّبُوهَا عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا ۚ كَانَ ذَٰلِكَ فِي الْكِتَابِ مَسْطُورًا
versets.tr: wa-in min qaryatin illā naḥnu muhlikūhā qabla yawmi l-qiyāmati aw muʿadhdhibūhā ʿadhāban shadīdan kāna dhālika fi l-kitābi masṭūran
versets.trad: There is no town but that We shall destroy it before the Day of Resurrection, or punish it with a severe punishment. That is written in the Book.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī moves the punishment from outside to within: the torment that strikes souls and outward forms is slight beside what strikes hearts and inmost secrets; the punishment of hearts, for the people of realities, is keener than what touches the people of poverty. And he recalls the law of return: whoever conveys to another a relief, the relief returns to him; whoever conveys to another a desolation, the desolation returns to him.

mots.def: the one who will make it perish.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ش د د

versets.ar: وَمَا مَنَعَنَا أَنْ نُرْسِلَ بِالْآيَاتِ إِلَّا أَنْ كَذَّبَ بِهَا الْأَوَّلُونَ ۚ وَآتَيْنَا ثَمُودَ النَّاقَةَ مُبْصِرَةً فَظَلَمُوا بِهَا ۚ وَمَا نُرْسِلُ بِالْآيَاتِ إِلَّا تَخْوِيفًا
versets.tr: wa-mā manaʿanā an nursila bi-l-āyāti illā an kadhdhaba bihā l-awwalūna wa-ātaynā thamūda n-nāqata mubṣiratan fa-ẓalamū bihā wa-mā nursilu bi-l-āyāti illā takhwīfan
versets.trad: Nothing prevents Us from sending signs save that the ancients denied them. We gave Thamūd the she-camel as a clear sign, but they wronged it. We send the signs only to instill fear.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sets out the divine custom: when a nation demanded a sign and did not believe after its appearance, the punishment was hastened against it; but it was decreed that the torment would not engulf the people of the Prophet's time, on account of those in their loins destined to believe — which is why the punishment they pressed for was deferred from them. And “We send signs only to make them fear”: the warning belongs to His beauty; if they do not fear, the punishment descends upon them.

mots.racine: ن و ق / ب ص ر
mots.sens: the she-camel as a clear sign

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لَكَ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ أَحَاطَ بِالنَّاسِ ۚ وَمَا جَعَلْنَا الرُّؤْيَا الَّتِي أَرَيْنَاكَ إِلَّا فِتْنَةً لِلنَّاسِ وَالشَّجَرَةَ الْمَلْعُونَةَ فِي الْقُرْآنِ ۚ وَنُخَوِّفُهُمْ فَمَا يَزِيدُهُمْ إِلَّا طُغْيَانًا كَبِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-idh qulnā laka inna rabbaka aḥāṭa bi-n-nāsi wa-mā jaʿalnā r-ruʾyā llatī araynāka illā fitnatan li-n-nāsi wa-sh-shajarata l-malʿūnata fi l-qurʾāni wa-nukhawwifuhum fa-mā yazīduhum illā ṭughyānan kabīran
versets.trad: When We said to you: “Your Lord encompasses mankind.” We made the vision We showed you only a trial for mankind, and likewise the tree cursed in the Qurʾān. We make them afraid, but it only increases them in great rebellion.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of the vision and the cursed tree (Zaqqūm) a sifting trial: faith in what We have singled you out for is a testing and a charge upon them, that the truthful be set apart from the hypocrite, the believer from the denier; those whom protection reached stood firm, were affirmed, believed and verified what was told them; but those in whose heart doubt dwelt, and whose secret the quintessence of oneness did not touch, gained from this trial only straying, perplexity and hardening.

mots.racine: ش ج ر / ل ع ن
ط غ ي / ك ب ر

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ اسْجُدُوا لِآدَمَ فَسَجَدُوا إِلَّا إِبْلِيسَ قَالَ أَأَسْجُدُ لِمَنْ خَلَقْتَ طِينًا
versets.tr: wa-idh qulnā li-l-malāʾikati sjudū li-ādama fa-sajadū illā iblīsa qāla a-asjudu li-man khalaqta ṭīnan
versets.trad: When We said to the angels: “Prostrate yourselves before Adam,” they prostrated, except Iblīs, who said: “Shall I prostrate before one You created of clay?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī probes the refusal of Iblīs: the wretch refused and said: “I do not prostrate before another than You with a face by which I have prostrated before You” — and this was ignorance on his part; for had he known God, he would have preferred His command and abandoned what his soul dictated. Iblīs did not lack worship, but knowledge: he set his own reasoning against the command.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ط ي ن

versets.ar: قَالَ أَرَأَيْتَكَ هَٰذَا الَّذِي كَرَّمْتَ عَلَيَّ لَئِنْ أَخَّرْتَنِ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ لَأَحْتَنِكَنَّ ذُرِّيَّتَهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا
versets.tr: qāla a-raʾaytaka hādhā lladhī karramta ʿalayya la-in akhkhartani ilā yawmi l-qiyāmati la-aḥtanikanna dhurriyyatahu illā qalīlan
versets.trad: He said: “Do You see this one whom You have honored above me? If You grant me respite until the Day of Resurrection, I will surely seize his descendants, save a few.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: had a single particle of knowledge and oneness clung to him, he would not have gathered against himself the firewood of straying and seduction; but the Real established him in that station and made him speak what, to the hearts of the people of verification, is manifest. The defiance of Iblīs is itself inscribed in the decree that tests mankind.

mots.def: to take by the bridle, to dominate.

versets.ar: قَالَ اذْهَبْ فَمَنْ تَبِعَكَ مِنْهُمْ فَإِنَّ جَهَنَّمَ جَزَاؤُكُمْ جَزَاءً مَوْفُورًا
versets.tr: qāla dhhab fa-man tabiʿaka minhum fa-inna jahannama jazāʾukum jazāʾan mawfūran
versets.trad: He said: “Be gone! Whoever of them follows you — Hell shall be your recompense, an ample recompense!”
versets.commentaire: “Be gone”: Qushayrī sees here the height of the threat: there is in it allusion and declaration that there is no escape; and if a people's punishment is deferred, it is a reprieve, not abandonment; a ruse and a gradual luring, not a favor or an honor. The granted delay is not grace but a slow snare.

mots.racine: ج ز ي / و ف ر

versets.ar: وَاسْتَفْزِزْ مَنِ اسْتَطَعْتَ مِنْهُمْ بِصَوْتِكَ وَأَجْلِبْ عَلَيْهِمْ بِخَيْلِكَ وَرَجِلِكَ وَشَارِكْهُمْ فِي الْأَمْوَالِ وَالْأَوْلَادِ وَعِدْهُمْ ۚ وَمَا يَعِدُهُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ إِلَّا غُرُورًا
versets.tr: wa-stafziz mani staṭaʿta minhum bi-ṣawtika wa-ajlib ʿalayhim bi-khaylika wa-rajilika wa-shārikhum fi l-amwāli wa-l-awlādi wa-ʿidhum wa-mā yaʿiduhumu sh-shayṭānu illā ghurūran
versets.trad: Stir up whomever of them you can with your voice, rally against them your horsemen and your footmen, share with them in wealth and children, and make them promises! Satan promises them nothing but delusion.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī defuses Satan's challenge by reducing it to impotence: “stir up whomever you can with your voice” means: do what you can, for your act has no effect on anyone — the Author and Originator is God. The permission given to the devil is but an unveiling of his nullity: he creates nothing, he only seduces at the surface.

mots.racine: خ ي ل / ر ج ل

versets.ar: إِنَّ عِبَادِي لَيْسَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَانٌ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِرَبِّكَ وَكِيلًا
versets.tr: inna ʿibādī laysa laka ʿalayhim sulṭānun wa-kafā bi-rabbika wakīlan
versets.trad: Truly over My servants you have no power. And your Lord suffices as guardian!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives several senses to “My servants”: in the general sense, none has an excuse-argument, for the argument belongs to God alone; nor can any real sway be ascribed to Iblīs, for what is under created power does not leave the domain of divine power. In the proper sense, they are the elect of the believers, people of keeping, mercy and care: the devil's whisperings do not harm them, for they take refuge in God unceasingly — and when the devil draws near the hearts of the knowers, he burns in the light of their knowledge; the devil's flight from the believer is stronger than the believer's flight from him.

mots.racine: ك ف ي / و ك ل

versets.ar: رَبُّكُمُ الَّذِي يُزْجِي لَكُمُ الْفُلْكَ فِي الْبَحْرِ لِتَبْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِهِ ۚ إِنَّهُ كَانَ بِكُمْ رَحِيمًا
versets.tr: rabbukumu lladhī yuzjī lakumu l-fulka fi l-baḥri li-tabtaghū min faḍlihi innahu kāna bikum raḥīman
versets.trad: Your Lord is He who drives the ship for you upon the sea, that you may seek of His bounty. Truly He is Merciful to you.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He has made Himself known to His servants by His creation and His blessings: there is no created thing — entity, trace, ruin or dust — that is not a witness to His oneness and a proof of His Lordship. The ship that sails is one of these countless signs that speak of the Benefactor.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا مَسَّكُمُ الضُّرُّ فِي الْبَحْرِ ضَلَّ مَنْ تَدْعُونَ إِلَّا إِيَّاهُ ۖ فَلَمَّا نَجَّاكُمْ إِلَى الْبَرِّ أَعْرَضْتُمْ ۚ وَكَانَ الْإِنْسَانُ كَفُورًا
versets.tr: wa-idhā massakumu ḍ-ḍurru fi l-baḥri ḍalla man tadʿūna illā iyyāhu fa-lammā najjākum ila l-barri aʿraḍtum wa-kāna l-insānu kafūran
versets.trad: When harm befalls you at sea, those you call upon vanish, save Him. But when He delivers you to land, you turn away. Man is ever ungrateful!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī describes the natural slope of man: man is so molded that, when a misfortune strikes him, he rushes to God to avert it, believing he will never again return to what does not please God; but as soon as God lifts the trial, they return to what they had repented of, as though they had never suffered the harm that touched them. Distress draws near, deliverance estranges — such is forgetful ingratitude.

mots.racine: م س س / ض ر ر
ض ل ل / د ع و

versets.ar: أَفَأَمِنْتُمْ أَنْ يَخْسِفَ بِكُمْ جَانِبَ الْبَرِّ أَوْ يُرْسِلَ عَلَيْكُمْ حَاصِبًا ثُمَّ لَا تَجِدُوا لَكُمْ وَكِيلًا
versets.tr: a-fa-amintum an yakhsifa bikum jāniba l-barri aw yursila ʿalaykum ḥāṣiban thumma lā tajidū lakum wakīlan
versets.trad: Do you then feel secure that He will not cause the shore to swallow you, or send against you a storm of stones, and then you find for yourselves no guardian?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of fear a vigilance of every breath: fear is the apprehension of punishments with each passing breath — so say the masters; and the most knowing of God is the most fearing of God. The false security of the delivered man is precisely what this verse comes to shatter: none is safe from the decree.

mots.def: to make the earth cave in.

versets.ar: أَمْ أَمِنْتُمْ أَنْ يُعِيدَكُمْ فِيهِ تَارَةً أُخْرَىٰ فَيُرْسِلَ عَلَيْكُمْ قَاصِفًا مِنَ الرِّيحِ فَيُغْرِقَكُمْ بِمَا كَفَرْتُمْ ۙ ثُمَّ لَا تَجِدُوا لَكُمْ عَلَيْنَا بِهِ تَبِيعًا
versets.tr: am amintum an yuʿīdakum fīhi tāratan ukhrā fa-yursila ʿalaykum qāṣifan mina r-rīḥi fa-yughriqakum bimā kafartum thumma lā tajidū lakum ʿalaynā bihi tabīʿan
versets.trad: Or do you feel secure that He will not send you back to it a second time, and send against you a hurricane of wind and drown you for your disbelief, and then you find no avenger for yourselves against Us?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints the alert state of the knowers: how many are joyful at the start of their night who wake in trial! how many afflicted, tossing on their beds, see at dawn the good tidings of the fullness of graces come to them! And he describes the people of knowledge: they stand ready, on one foot, as though wishing to depart and break camp — ever watchful of the decree.

mots.racine: ق ص ف / ر و ح

mots.def: one who claims on your behalf.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ كَرَّمْنَا بَنِي آدَمَ وَحَمَلْنَاهُمْ فِي الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ وَرَزَقْنَاهُمْ مِنَ الطَّيِّبَاتِ وَفَضَّلْنَاهُمْ عَلَىٰ كَثِيرٍ مِمَّنْ خَلَقْنَا تَفْضِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-laqad karramnā banī ādama wa-ḥamalnāhum fi l-barri wa-l-baḥri wa-razaqnāhum mina ṭ-ṭayyibāti wa-faḍḍalnāhum ʿalā kathīrin mimman khalaqnā tafḍīlan
versets.trad: We have honored the children of Adam, carried them on land and sea, provided them with good things, and favored them above much of what We created.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a summit: He said “We have honored the children of Adam” and not “the believers” or “the worshippers,” to show that honor is not the counterpart of an act nor caused by a merit that would require it. He tells over the marks of this gratuitous honor: that they may, whenever they wish, stand with Him on the carpet of confidential discourse; that He accepts their repentance even when repeated; that He gives them before they ask and forgives before they implore — “I gave you before you asked Me, and I forgave you before you sought My forgiveness”; and that He said to them “Remember Me, I will remember you,” which He said neither to the angels nor to the jinn.

mots.racine: ك ر م / ب ن ي
mots.sens: We have honored the children of Adam

versets.ar: يَوْمَ نَدْعُو كُلَّ أُنَاسٍ بِإِمَامِهِمْ ۖ فَمَنْ أُوتِيَ كِتَابَهُ بِيَمِينِهِ فَأُولَٰئِكَ يَقْرَءُونَ كِتَابَهُمْ وَلَا يُظْلَمُونَ فَتِيلًا
versets.tr: yawma nadʿū kulla unāsin bi-imāmihim fa-man ūtiya kitābahu bi-yamīnihi fa-ulāʾika yaqraʾūna kitābahum wa-lā yuẓlamūna fatīlan
versets.trad: The Day We call every people by their leader: those given their book in their right hand will read their book and will not be wronged by so much as a thread.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī meditates on the “imām” each one follows: each one's imām is the one he takes as model; but there is an imām by whom the follower is guided, and an imām by whom the follower is destroyed. As for those who receive their book in the right hand, they read their book by the perfection of their lucidity and the mastery of their intellect; while those whose book is not handed to the right do not read it, out of their dread and confusion.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ي م ن
mots.sens: given his book in his right

versets.ar: وَمَنْ كَانَ فِي هَٰذِهِ أَعْمَىٰ فَهُوَ فِي الْآخِرَةِ أَعْمَىٰ وَأَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-man kāna fī hādhihi aʿmā fa-huwa fi l-ākhirati aʿmā wa-aḍallu sabīlan
versets.trad: Whoever is blind in this world will be blind in the Hereafter, and even more astray from the way.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī specifies the nature of this blindness: in the Hereafter, blind to beholding Him with his inner sight; and his punishment in the Hereafter is separation, to which is added the burning — that is why he is “the more astray”. The worst blindness is not that of the eyes but of the heart that does not see God.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / س ب ل

versets.ar: وَإِنْ كَادُوا لَيَفْتِنُونَكَ عَنِ الَّذِي أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْكَ لِتَفْتَرِيَ عَلَيْنَا غَيْرَهُ ۖ وَإِذًا لَاتَّخَذُوكَ خَلِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-in kādū la-yaftinūnaka ʿani lladhī awḥaynā ilayka li-taftariya ʿalaynā ghayrahu wa-idhan la-ttakhadhūka khalīlan
versets.trad: They nearly tempted you away from what We revealed to you, that you might fabricate against Us something else; and then they would have taken you as a friend.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī affirms the preservation (ʿiṣma) of the Prophet: We raised about you the pavilions of safekeeping, sheltered you in the bosom of Our protection, and guarded you from the peril of following your inclination; the lapse, on your part, is impossible, and fabrication in your description is not conceivable. Were you to incline an instant toward divergence, the rigors of the trial would redouble upon you, because of the perfection of your rank — for whoever is highest in degree, his fault, were it to occur, would have the gravest effect.

mots.racine: ك و د / ف ت ن

versets.ar: وَلَوْلَا أَنْ ثَبَّتْنَاكَ لَقَدْ كِدْتَ تَرْكَنُ إِلَيْهِمْ شَيْئًا قَلِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-lawlā an thabbatnāka laqad kidta tarkanu ilayhim shayʾan qalīlan
versets.trad: Had We not made you firm, you would nearly have inclined toward them a little.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: had We entrusted you to yourself and lifted from you the shade of preservation, you would have brushed against something of what is not permitted, contrary to Our command; but We singled you out by the keeping, whose traces never fail you and whose lights never depart from your courtyard. The divine steadfastness precedes and makes the fall impossible.

mots.sens: had We not made you firm

mots.def: to be on the point of.

versets.ar: إِذًا لَأَذَقْنَاكَ ضِعْفَ الْحَيَاةِ وَضِعْفَ الْمَمَاتِ ثُمَّ لَا تَجِدُ لَكَ عَلَيْنَا نَصِيرًا
versets.tr: idhan la-adhaqnāka ḍiʿfa l-ḥayāti wa-ḍiʿfa l-mamāti thumma lā tajidu laka ʿalaynā naṣīran
versets.trad: Then We would have made you taste a double of life and a double of death, and you would have found no helper against Us.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī states a law of nearness: the fall of the greatest is in measure of their elevation, and the trials of the beloved, however slight, are immense. The higher the rank, the more severely the least inclination would be punished — not from harshness, but because love demands entire purity.

mots.sens: We would have made you taste

mots.racine: ض ع ف / ح ي ي

versets.ar: وَإِنْ كَادُوا لَيَسْتَفِزُّونَكَ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ لِيُخْرِجُوكَ مِنْهَا ۖ وَإِذًا لَا يَلْبَثُونَ خِلَافَكَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-in kādū la-yastafizzūnaka mina l-arḍi li-yukhrijūka minhā wa-idhan lā yalbathūna khilāfaka illā qalīlan
versets.trad: They nearly drove you from the land to expel you from it; but then they would have remained after you only a little,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever thinks to enjoy his life after the departure of the dear and the great is mistaken in his reckoning; and the envier does not reign. Then he widens to the freedom of God's friends: the whole earth is Our kingdom; We make Our friends circulate in their wanderings through the lands, a strolling upon Our carpet and a movement in Our dwellings — the regions are alike to them.

versets.ar: سُنَّةَ مَنْ قَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا قَبْلَكَ مِنْ رُسُلِنَا ۖ وَلَا تَجِدُ لِسُنَّتِنَا تَحْوِيلًا
versets.tr: sunnata man qad arsalnā qablaka min rusulinā wa-lā tajidu li-sunnatinā taḥwīlan
versets.trad: according to the way We dealt with the messengers We sent before you; and you will find no change in Our way.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Real has carried out His custom with His friends through favor, and with His enemies through abasement; and no change affects either one. The divine sunna is constant: grace for the near, abasement for the rebellious.

versets.ar: أَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ لِدُلُوكِ الشَّمْسِ إِلَىٰ غَسَقِ اللَّيْلِ وَقُرْآنَ الْفَجْرِ ۖ إِنَّ قُرْآنَ الْفَجْرِ كَانَ مَشْهُودًا
versets.tr: aqimi ṣ-ṣalāta li-dulūki sh-shamsi ilā ghasaqi l-layli wa-qurʾāna l-fajri inna qurʾāna l-fajri kāna mashhūdan
versets.trad: Establish the prayer from the decline of the sun until the darkness of the night, and the recitation of dawn, for the recitation of dawn is witnessed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī raises the definition of prayer: prayer is to knock at the door of provision; it is the standing in the place of confidential discourse; it is the retreat of the heart into the sanctuaries of the decree; it is the halt upon the carpet of secret converse. He explains the dispersing of the hours: the times of prayer are spread out so that the servant may return to that carpet several times by day and night. And the dawn has as its witnesses the angels of night and day.

mots.racine: ق و م / ص ل و
د ل ك / ش م س
mots.sens: at the decline of the sun

versets.ar: وَمِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَتَهَجَّدْ بِهِ نَافِلَةً لَكَ عَسَىٰ أَنْ يَبْعَثَكَ رَبُّكَ مَقَامًا مَحْمُودًا
versets.tr: wa-mina l-layli fa-tahajjad bihi nāfilatan laka ʿasā an yabʿathaka rabbuka maqāman maḥmūdan
versets.trad: And keep vigil in part of the night, as a supererogatory work for you; perhaps your Lord will raise you to a praised station.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī apportions the night among three sorts of men: it is for the seekers of salvation — the sinners who incline to repentance; or for the people of degrees — who strive in obediences and race in good; or for the people of confidence with the Beloved, at the hour when men are in their heedlessness and absence. And the “praised station” (maqām maḥmūd), he says, is the converse in the state of contemplation; some say: contemplation itself; some say: the intercession for the sinners; or that with which the Prophet will be singled out, which none will share with him.

mots.racine: ق و م / ح م د

versets.ar: وَقُلْ رَبِّ أَدْخِلْنِي مُدْخَلَ صِدْقٍ وَأَخْرِجْنِي مُخْرَجَ صِدْقٍ وَاجْعَلْ لِي مِنْ لَدُنْكَ سُلْطَانًا نَصِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-qul rabbi adkhilnī mudkhala ṣidqin wa-akhrijnī mukhraja ṣidqin wa-jʿal lī min ladunka sulṭānan naṣīran
versets.trad: And say: “My Lord, cause me to enter with a true entrance and to leave with a true departure, and grant me from Yourself a victorious authority.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī defines the “ṣidq” of entrance and departure: truthfulness is that his entrance into things be through God, for God, and not for another; and that his departure from things be through God, for God, and not for another. And the “victorious authority”: that I may regard neither my entrance nor my departure — the effacement of self in the divine acting.

mots.racine: د خ ل / ص د ق
mots.def: to enter through and for God.

mots.racine: خ ر ج / ص د ق
mots.def: to leave through and for God.

mots.racine: س ل ط / ن ص ر

versets.ar: وَقُلْ جَاءَ الْحَقُّ وَزَهَقَ الْبَاطِلُ ۚ إِنَّ الْبَاطِلَ كَانَ زَهُوقًا
versets.tr: wa-qul jāʾa l-ḥaqqu wa-zahaqa l-bāṭilu inna l-bāṭila kāna zahūqan
versets.trad: And say: “Truth has come and falsehood has vanished. Truly falsehood is ever bound to vanish.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the senses of al-Ḥaqq: the absolute Real is the true Existent, the One who possesses the right and who realizes the true; the relative true is what is sound in belief, act and word; the false is its opposite. And he interiorizes: the true, among the thoughts that cross the heart, is what calls to God; the false, what calls to other than God. Before the coming of the Real, the false can only vanish.

mots.racine: ج ي أ / ح ق ق
ز ه ق / ب ط ل

versets.ar: وَنُنَزِّلُ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ مَا هُوَ شِفَاءٌ وَرَحْمَةٌ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۙ وَلَا يَزِيدُ الظَّالِمِينَ إِلَّا خَسَارًا
versets.tr: wa-nunazzilu mina l-qurʾāni mā huwa shifāʾun wa-raḥmatun li-l-muʾminīna wa-lā yazīdu ẓ-ẓālimīna illā khasāran
versets.trad: We send down of the Qurʾān that which is a healing and a mercy for the believers, but for the wrongdoers it only increases their loss.
themes: the double face of the Word
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the healings of the Qurʾān: it heals the learned of the malady of ignorance, the believers of the malady of associationism, the knowers of the malady of unknowing, the lovers of the stings of longing, and the aspirants of the malady of excess. But it is one and the same speech, one and the same Book: for some mercy and healing, for others wrath and misery — a people whose inner sights He has illumined with the light of oneness, and a people whose inner sights He has veiled with the screen of denial.

mots.racine: ش ف ي / ر ح م

versets.ar: وَإِذَا أَنْعَمْنَا عَلَى الْإِنْسَانِ أَعْرَضَ وَنَأَىٰ بِجَانِبِهِ ۖ وَإِذَا مَسَّهُ الشَّرُّ كَانَ يَئُوسًا
versets.tr: wa-idhā anʿamnā ʿala l-insāni aʿraḍa wa-naʾā bi-jānibihi wa-idhā massahu sh-sharru kāna yaʾūsan
versets.trad: When We bestow favor upon man, he turns away and draws aside; and when evil touches him, he despairs.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī probes the turning-away of the man favored: when We remove from him the motives of fear, loosen for him the rein of respite, and prepare for him the causes of ease, the illusions of forgetfulness seize him, the calls of revolt prevail, he turns from gratitude and withdraws from the carpet of accord. And he points to the root of this straying: he sees the grace as coming from himself and not from the Real, imagining that the favors he has are owed to the merit of an obedience he has purified or a trial he has endured — and this, in truth, is a form of associationism.

versets.ar: قُلْ كُلٌّ يَعْمَلُ عَلَىٰ شَاكِلَتِهِ فَرَبُّكُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنْ هُوَ أَهْدَىٰ سَبِيلًا
versets.tr: qul kullun yaʿmalu ʿalā shākilatihi fa-rabbukum aʿlamu bi-man huwa ahdā sabīlan
versets.trad: Say: “Each acts according to his own way; but your Lord knows best who is best guided in the path.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes “each acts according to his form” a law of transparency: each one streams from what he holds within; the faces indicate the inmost self, and what the consciences conceal surfaces upon the secrets; he whose essence is pure of all trouble exhales only the fragrance of his virtues, and he whose nature is kneaded with trouble — one smells around him only the odor of his flaws. The act reveals the hidden substance.

mots.sens: according to his form, his nature

mots.racine: ه د ي / س ب ل

versets.ar: وَيَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الرُّوحِ ۖ قُلِ الرُّوحُ مِنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّي وَمَا أُوتِيتُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-yasʾalūnaka ʿani r-rūḥi quli r-rūḥu min amri rabbī wa-mā ūtītum mina l-ʿilmi illā qalīlan
versets.trad: They ask you about the Spirit. Say: “The Spirit is of the command of my Lord, and you have been given of knowledge but little.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī defines the servant's spirit: the spirit is a subtle reality God has deposited in the vessel of the body and made the seat of the subtle states and the noble virtues; as the eye is the locus of vision and the ear of hearing, the spirit is the locus of the praiseworthy qualities, while the soul (nafs) is the locus of the blamed qualities. He adds: the spirit is created, of a subtlety whose purity surpasses that of all, and the “little knowledge” is because none has ever beheld the spirit with his sight.

mots.sens: of the command of my Lord

versets.ar: وَلَئِنْ شِئْنَا لَنَذْهَبَنَّ بِالَّذِي أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْكَ ثُمَّ لَا تَجِدُ لَكَ بِهِ عَلَيْنَا وَكِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-la-in shiʾnā la-nadhhabanna bi-lladhī awḥaynā ilayka thumma lā tajidu laka bihi ʿalaynā wakīlan
versets.trad: If We willed, We could take away what We have revealed to you, and then you would find for yourself none to plead your cause against Us,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a school of poverty (iftiqār): the custom of the Real toward His beloved and the elect of His servants is to perpetuate their need of Him, so that in every state they remain submitted to the course of His decree, and no fiber stir in them against His choice. Even to the Prophet God recalls that the revelation is pure gift, that dependence may remain entire.

mots.def: one who would plead your cause.

versets.ar: إِلَّا رَحْمَةً مِنْ رَبِّكَ ۚ إِنَّ فَضْلَهُ كَانَ عَلَيْكَ كَبِيرًا
versets.tr: illā raḥmatan min rabbika inna faḍlahu kāna ʿalayka kabīran
versets.trad: except as a mercy from your Lord; truly His favor toward you is great.
versets.commentaire: The permanence of the revelation is but mercy. Qushayrī: the aim is to perpetuate the singularity of the Prophet's secret with Him alone, to the exclusion of all other. The grace that sustains the gift sustains too the exclusive bond of the beloved to the Loving.

versets.ar: قُلْ لَئِنِ اجْتَمَعَتِ الْإِنْسُ وَالْجِنُّ عَلَىٰ أَنْ يَأْتُوا بِمِثْلِ هَٰذَا الْقُرْآنِ لَا يَأْتُونَ بِمِثْلِهِ وَلَوْ كَانَ بَعْضُهُمْ لِبَعْضٍ ظَهِيرًا
versets.tr: qul la-ini jtamaʿati l-insu wa-l-jinnu ʿalā an yaʾtū bi-mithli hādhā l-qurʾāni lā yaʾtūna bi-mithlihi wa-law kāna baʿḍuhum li-baʿḍin ẓahīran
versets.trad: Say: “If mankind and the jinn gathered to produce the like of this Qurʾān, they could not produce its like, even if they backed one another.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the Qurʾān the living miracle: the miracles of the other prophets endure through the effect of the report, but the miracle of our Prophet endures in its very essence: it is the Qurʾān, which we recite, and which falsehood reaches neither from before nor from behind. Inimitability (iʿjāz) is the proof perpetually present.

mots.racine: ج م ع / أ ن س / ج ن ن
م ث ل / ق ر أ

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ صَرَّفْنَا لِلنَّاسِ فِي هَٰذَا الْقُرْآنِ مِنْ كُلِّ مَثَلٍ فَأَبَىٰ أَكْثَرُ النَّاسِ إِلَّا كُفُورًا
versets.tr: wa-laqad ṣarrafnā li-n-nāsi fī hādhā l-qurʾāni min kulli mathalin fa-abā aktharu n-nāsi illā kufūran
versets.trad: We have set forth for mankind in this Qurʾān every kind of example, yet most people refuse all but disbelief.
versets.commentaire: God has varied every kind of example, yet obstinacy remains. Qushayrī sings the love of the Book despite the refusal of the many: nothing is more precious to the beloved than the book of the beloved; it is a healing for the malady of languor, and a light for their secrets when the trial intensifies.
