The Word remains a treasure for the lover, though rejected by the crowd.

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّىٰ تَفْجُرَ لَنَا مِنَ الْأَرْضِ يَنْبُوعًا
versets.tr: wa-qālū lan nuʾmina laka ḥattā tafjura lanā mina l-arḍi yanbūʿan
versets.trad: They say: “We will not believe you until you make a spring gush forth for us from the earth,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they demanded signs after every excuse had been removed and every need met; they ran in the arena of bad adab and deprived themselves of union and nearness. And even were they granted what they asked, they would only gain increased denial and unknowing. The demand for prodigies was no quest for truth but a flight.

mots.sens: you make gush forth for us

versets.ar: أَوْ تَكُونَ لَكَ جَنَّةٌ مِنْ نَخِيلٍ وَعِنَبٍ فَتُفَجِّرَ الْأَنْهَارَ خِلَالَهَا تَفْجِيرًا
versets.tr: aw takūna laka jannatun min nakhīlin wa-ʿinabin fa-tufajjira l-anhāra khilālahā tafjīran
versets.trad: or until you have a garden of palms and vines, and make rivers gush abundantly through it,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, pursuing the refutation of the demands, recalls the generosity of the Real set against the fickleness of the deniers: the generous one, when he gratifies you with his affection, covers the ugly and brings forth the good; but the inconstant, when he wishes to break off, wearies of union and invokes a thousand pretexts. The demands of the disbelievers are but pretexts of a rupture already resolved.

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ن خ ل / ع ن ب
mots.sens: a garden of palms and vines

mots.racine: ف ج ر / ن ه ر

versets.ar: أَوْ تُسْقِطَ السَّمَاءَ كَمَا زَعَمْتَ عَلَيْنَا كِسَفًا أَوْ تَأْتِيَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْمَلَائِكَةِ قَبِيلًا
versets.tr: aw tusqiṭa s-samāʾa kamā zaʿamta ʿalaynā kisafan aw taʾtiya bi-llāhi wa-l-malāʾikati qabīlan
versets.trad: or until you make the sky fall upon us in pieces, as you have claimed, or bring God and the angels before us face to face,
versets.commentaire: The demands mount to blasphemy: to bring God and the angels “face to face.” Qushayrī sees in this escalation the sign of a closed heart: no sign, however prodigious, converts one who has already chosen refusal. Were they granted what they ask, they would gain only denial — for the evil comes not from lack of proof but from the heart's aversion.

mots.racine: س ق ط / س م و

versets.ar: أَوْ يَكُونَ لَكَ بَيْتٌ مِنْ زُخْرُفٍ أَوْ تَرْقَىٰ فِي السَّمَاءِ وَلَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لِرُقِيِّكَ حَتَّىٰ تُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْنَا كِتَابًا نَقْرَؤُهُ ۗ قُلْ سُبْحَانَ رَبِّي هَلْ كُنْتُ إِلَّا بَشَرًا رَسُولًا
versets.tr: aw yakūna laka baytun min zukhrufin aw tarqā fi s-samāʾi wa-lan nuʾmina li-ruqiyyika ḥattā tunazzila ʿalaynā kitāban naqraʾuhu qul subḥāna rabbī hal kuntu illā basharan rasūlan
versets.trad: or until you have a house adorned with gold, or you ascend into the sky; and we will not believe in your ascent until you bring down upon us a book that we may read.” Say: “Glory to my Lord! Am I anything but a mortal, a messenger?”
versets.commentaire: To the avalanche of demands the Prophet answers with glorification and the confession of his servanthood. Qushayrī comments: say, O Muḥammad: Glory to my Lord! Whence should I have, of myself, the power to produce what you demand? Is my quality other than servanthood? Am I anything but a human? — as God said: “The Messiah does not disdain to be a servant of God.” The summit of prophetic greatness is the confession of pure ʿubūdiyya.

mots.racine: ب ي ت / ز خ ر ف
ر ق ي / س م و
ب ش ر / ر س ل
mots.def: a human invested with a message.

versets.ar: وَمَا مَنَعَ النَّاسَ أَنْ يُؤْمِنُوا إِذْ جَاءَهُمُ الْهُدَىٰ إِلَّا أَنْ قَالُوا أَبَعَثَ اللَّهُ بَشَرًا رَسُولًا
versets.tr: wa-mā manaʿa n-nāsa an yuʾminū idh jāʾahumu l-hudā illā an qālū a-baʿatha llāhu basharan rasūlan
versets.trad: Nothing prevents people from believing when guidance comes to them but that they say: “Has God sent a mortal as a messenger?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they marveled at what is no place for doubt; but what drove them to it was the excess of their ignorance, and then they persisted in their denial and rejection. The sending of a human to humans is in no way improbable; the scandal is only in their hearts.

mots.racine: ج ي أ / ه د ي
ب ع ث / ب ش ر

versets.ar: قُلْ لَوْ كَانَ فِي الْأَرْضِ مَلَائِكَةٌ يَمْشُونَ مُطْمَئِنِّينَ لَنَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَلَكًا رَسُولًا
versets.tr: qul law kāna fi l-arḍi malāʾikatun yamshūna muṭmaʾinnīna la-nazzalnā ʿalayhim mina s-samāʾi malakan rasūlan
versets.trad: Say: “Were there angels walking at ease upon the earth, We would have sent down upon them from heaven an angel as a messenger.”
themes: the messenger of the same kind
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī states a law of fittingness: kind inclines to kind, and the like finds its intimacy in the like; God therefore said: were the inhabitants of the earth angels, We would have made for them an angel as messenger; but since they are humans, the sending of a human to humans is not to be deemed improbable. The nearness of nature makes teaching possible.

mots.racine: م ل ك / م ش ي
م ل ك / ر س ل

versets.ar: قُلْ كَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ شَهِيدًا بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُ كَانَ بِعِبَادِهِ خَبِيرًا بَصِيرًا
versets.tr: qul kafā bi-llāhi shahīdan baynī wa-baynakum innahu kāna bi-ʿibādihi khabīran baṣīran
versets.trad: Say: “God suffices as witness between me and you. He is Aware and Seeing of His servants.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Real is the Judge and the Witness all at once; and His judgment is not measured by that of creatures: it is not admissible, in the quality of the created, that the judge be also the witness — but just as His Essence does not resemble the essence of creatures, His attribute does not resemble their attribute. God alone can be at once judge and witness.

mots.racine: ك ف ي / ش ه د
خ ب ر / ب ص ر

versets.ar: وَمَنْ يَهْدِ اللَّهُ فَهُوَ الْمُهْتَدِ ۖ وَمَنْ يُضْلِلْ فَلَنْ تَجِدَ لَهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءَ مِنْ دُونِهِ ۖ وَنَحْشُرُهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ عَلَىٰ وُجُوهِهِمْ عُمْيًا وَبُكْمًا وَصُمًّا ۖ مَأْوَاهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ ۖ كُلَّمَا خَبَتْ زِدْنَاهُمْ سَعِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-man yahdi llāhu fa-huwa l-muhtadi wa-man yuḍlil fa-lan tajida lahum awliyāʾa min dūnihi wa-naḥshuruhum yawma l-qiyāmati ʿalā wujūhihim ʿumyan wa-bukman wa-ṣumman maʾwāhum jahannamu kullamā khabat zidnāhum saʿīran
versets.trad: Whomever God guides is rightly guided. And for those He leads astray, you will find no protectors besides Him. On the Day of Resurrection We shall gather them upon their faces, blind, mute and deaf. Their abode is Hell; whenever it abates, We increase for them the blazing flame.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī refers everything to the eternal precedence: the one He willed for felicity in His pre-eternity, He elected in His post-eternity by His bounties; and the one He knew for misery in the pre-eternal, He marked in the post-eternal with the seal of the enemies. No change affects His judgment, no substitution His word.

mots.racine: ع م ي / ب ك م / ص م م
mots.def: deprived of the senses of salvation.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ جَزَاؤُهُمْ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَفَرُوا بِآيَاتِنَا وَقَالُوا أَإِذَا كُنَّا عِظَامًا وَرُفَاتًا أَإِنَّا لَمَبْعُوثُونَ خَلْقًا جَدِيدًا
versets.tr: dhālika jazāʾuhum bi-annahum kafarū bi-āyātinā wa-qālū a-idhā kunnā ʿiẓāman wa-rufātan a-innā la-mabʿūthūna khalqan jadīdan
versets.trad: That is their recompense for disbelieving in Our signs and saying: “When we are bones and dust, shall we really be raised up in a new creation?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses the link between obstinacy and penalty: when they persisted in their denial, the Real repaid them by perpetuating their punishment; had the divine aid seconded them, verification would have been found in them; but they were deprived of the divine support and so deprived of oneness. The denial of resurrection is the fruit of a heart abandoned to itself.

mots.racine: ك ف ر / أ ي ي
ب ع ث / خ ل ق

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّ اللَّهَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ قَادِرٌ عَلَىٰ أَنْ يَخْلُقَ مِثْلَهُمْ وَجَعَلَ لَهُمْ أَجَلًا لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ فَأَبَى الظَّالِمُونَ إِلَّا كُفُورًا
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yaraw anna llāha lladhī khalaqa s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa qādirun ʿalā an yakhluqa mithlahum wa-jaʿala lahum ajalan lā rayba fīhi fa-abā ẓ-ẓālimūna illā kufūran
versets.trad: Do they not see that God, who created the heavens and the earth, is able to create their like? He has set for them a term in which there is no doubt; yet the wrongdoers persist in nothing but disbelief.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees in this verse a foundation of analogy (qiyās): God has here paved the way for establishing reasoning by analogy; He has left in the Book no rule of religion without supporting it by proof and evidence, so that all have known that to rely on blind imitation is error and straying itself. He who created the heavens can recreate man: sound reason sees it.

mots.racine: ق د ر / خ ل ق
أ ج ل / ر ي ب

versets.ar: قُلْ لَوْ أَنْتُمْ تَمْلِكُونَ خَزَائِنَ رَحْمَةِ رَبِّي إِذًا لَأَمْسَكْتُمْ خَشْيَةَ الْإِنْفَاقِ ۚ وَكَانَ الْإِنْسَانُ قَتُورًا
versets.tr: qul law antum tamlikūna khazāʾina raḥmati rabbī idhan la-amsaktum khashyata l-infāqi wa-kāna l-insānu qatūran
versets.trad: Say: “If you possessed the treasuries of my Lord's mercy, you would withhold them for fear of spending.” Man is ever niggardly!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: avarice is the instinct of man, and miserliness his temperament — to the point that even masters of the treasuries of divine mercy would withhold them for fear of exhausting them. The contrast bursts forth with the liberality of God, whose treasuries no spending diminishes.

mots.racine: خ ز ن / ر ح م
mots.sens: the treasuries of my Lord's mercy

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا مُوسَىٰ تِسْعَ آيَاتٍ بَيِّنَاتٍ ۖ فَاسْأَلْ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ إِذْ جَاءَهُمْ فَقَالَ لَهُ فِرْعَوْنُ إِنِّي لَأَظُنُّكَ يَا مُوسَىٰ مَسْحُورًا
versets.tr: wa-laqad ātaynā mūsā tisʿa āyātin bayyinātin fa-sʾal banī isrāʾīla idh jāʾahum fa-qāla lahu firʿawnu innī la-aẓunnuka yā mūsā masḥūran
versets.trad: We gave Moses nine clear signs; ask the Children of Israel. When he came to them, Pharaoh said to him: “O Moses, I think you are bewitched!”
versets.commentaire: The nine signs given to Moses are, says Qushayrī, the marks of his dignity and the signs of the love God bears him. Where Pharaoh sees only sorcery, the knower sees moments of election: the same prodigy is for one a proof of divine love, for the other a pretext for denial.

mots.racine: ت س ع / أ ي ي / ب ي ن
ظ ن ن / س ح ر

versets.ar: قَالَ لَقَدْ عَلِمْتَ مَا أَنْزَلَ هَٰؤُلَاءِ إِلَّا رَبُّ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ بَصَائِرَ وَإِنِّي لَأَظُنُّكَ يَا فِرْعَوْنُ مَثْبُورًا
versets.tr: qāla laqad ʿalimta mā anzala hāʾulāʾi illā rabbu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi baṣāʾira wa-innī la-aẓunnuka yā firʿawnu mathbūran
versets.trad: He said: “You know full well that none sent down these but the Lord of the heavens and the earth, as clear proofs. And I think you, O Pharaoh, doomed to ruin!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unmasks Pharaoh's lucid denial: you, O Pharaoh, followed the path of reasoning and knew that such things can come only from God; but you leaned upon heedlessness, in the darkness of ignorance. Pharaoh does not lack knowledge: he refuses against his own knowledge — this is ruin (thubūr).

versets.ar: فَأَرَادَ أَنْ يَسْتَفِزَّهُمْ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ فَأَغْرَقْنَاهُ وَمَنْ مَعَهُ جَمِيعًا
versets.tr: fa-arāda an yastafizzahum mina l-arḍi fa-aghraqnāhu wa-man maʿahu jamīʿan
versets.trad: He sought to drive them from the land, but We drowned him and all who were with him.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opposes the design of the creature to the will of the Real: Pharaoh willed to annihilate the Children of Israel and uproot them, and the Real willed their help and survival — and it was what the Real willed, not what the accursed one schemed. When the will of God and the calculation of the enemy clash, it is always the decree that prevails.

mots.racine: ر و د / ف ز ز
mots.sens: he sought to drive them out
and all who were with him

versets.ar: وَقُلْنَا مِنْ بَعْدِهِ لِبَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ اسْكُنُوا الْأَرْضَ فَإِذَا جَاءَ وَعْدُ الْآخِرَةِ جِئْنَا بِكُمْ لَفِيفًا
versets.tr: wa-qulnā min baʿdihi li-banī isrāʾīla skunū l-arḍa fa-idhā jāʾa waʿdu l-ākhirati jiʾnā bikum lafīfan
versets.trad: After him We said to the Children of Israel: “Dwell in the land, and when the promise of the Hereafter comes, We shall bring you all together.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He made them inherit the dwellings of their enemies, gave them power over their treasures and homes, and enjoined upon them gratitude for His grace; He warned them that if they followed, in disobedience, the path of those before them, they would taste the same punishment. The inheritance of the land is a trust that obliges to gratitude.

mots.racine: س ك ن / أ ر ض
و ع د / أ خ ر

versets.ar: وَبِالْحَقِّ أَنْزَلْنَاهُ وَبِالْحَقِّ نَزَلَ ۗ وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّرًا وَنَذِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-bi-l-ḥaqqi anzalnāhu wa-bi-l-ḥaqqi nazala wa-mā arsalnāka illā mubashshiran wa-nadhīran
versets.trad: With the truth We sent it down, and with the truth it descended. We have sent you only as a bearer of good tidings and a warner.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī seals the Qurʾān's truthfulness on every side: the Qurʾān is true, its descent is by the true, the One who sends it down is true, and the one upon whom it descends is true: it descended by the true, from the True, upon the true. The Prophet is sent only to announce and to warn — bearer of the Real, not a coercer.

mots.racine: ح ق ق / ن ز ل
mots.sens: with the truth We sent it down

mots.racine: ح ق ق / ن ز ل
mots.def: a descent conformed to the true.

mots.racine: ب ش ر / ن ذ ر

versets.ar: وَقُرْآنًا فَرَقْنَاهُ لِتَقْرَأَهُ عَلَى النَّاسِ عَلَىٰ مُكْثٍ وَنَزَّلْنَاهُ تَنْزِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-qurʾānan faraqnāhu li-taqraʾahu ʿala n-nāsi ʿalā mukthin wa-nazzalnāhu tanzīlan
versets.trad: A Qurʾān We have divided, that you may recite it to people at intervals; and We have sent it down by gradual revelation.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives the reasons for the fragmentation of the Qurʾān: the Qurʾān was divided to ease its memorization for the Prophet, to multiply the comings and goings of the heavenly messenger to him from his Lord, and that its descent, at each moment and each event, might be a proof that it is not the work of another who helped him with it. The gradual descent is itself a sign of authenticity.

mots.racine: ق ر أ / ف ر ق

versets.ar: قُلْ آمِنُوا بِهِ أَوْ لَا تُؤْمِنُوا ۚ إِنَّ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ إِذَا يُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ يَخِرُّونَ لِلْأَذْقَانِ سُجَّدًا
versets.tr: qul āminū bihi aw lā tuʾminū inna lladhīna ūtū l-ʿilma min qablihi idhā yutlā ʿalayhim yakhirrūna li-l-adhqāni sujjadan
versets.trad: Say: “Believe in it or do not believe; truly those given knowledge before it, when it is recited to them, fall down upon their faces in prostration,”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if you believe, the profit is for you; if you deny, the faith of Our friends who believe makes up for you, and the harm falls back upon you. And he describes the illumined: those over whom We have raised the suns of Our attention, whose lights of knowledge shine forth — when Our verses are recited to them, they prostrate instead of denying, respond instead of rebelling, and meet with assent what is said to them.

mots.sens: believe in it or do not

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ع ل م
خ ر ر / ذ ق ن / س ج د
mots.sens: they fall prostrate on their chins

versets.ar: وَيَقُولُونَ سُبْحَانَ رَبِّنَا إِنْ كَانَ وَعْدُ رَبِّنَا لَمَفْعُولًا
versets.tr: wa-yaqūlūna subḥāna rabbinā in kāna waʿdu rabbinā la-mafʿūlan
versets.trad: and they say: “Glory to our Lord! The promise of our Lord is surely fulfilled!”
versets.commentaire: The knowers welcome the Word with glorification: “Glory to our Lord!” Qushayrī places them in the line of the illumined hearts who, before the recitation, prostrate instead of denying and meet it with assent. Their tasbīḥ attests that God's promise — the coming of the Truth — has been realized in them.

mots.racine: س ب ح / ر ب ب

versets.ar: وَيَخِرُّونَ لِلْأَذْقَانِ يَبْكُونَ وَيَزِيدُهُمْ خُشُوعًا ۩
versets.tr: wa-yakhirrūna li-l-adhqāni yabkūna wa-yazīduhum khushūʿan
versets.trad: And they fall down upon their faces weeping, and it increases them in humility.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives a typology of tears: each weeps according to his state: the penitent weeps from fear of punishment for his past faults; the obedient weeps for his insufficiency in service, and from fear of missing the grace he hopes for; others weep from uncertainty about their end; others still without assignable cause; and others from regret for what escapes them of the Real. The audition of the Word acts variously: through insight in the learned, through dazzlement in the unified.

mots.racine: ز ي د / خ ش ع

versets.ar: قُلِ ادْعُوا اللَّهَ أَوِ ادْعُوا الرَّحْمَٰنَ ۖ أَيًّا مَا تَدْعُوا فَلَهُ الْأَسْمَاءُ الْحُسْنَىٰ ۚ وَلَا تَجْهَرْ بِصَلَاتِكَ وَلَا تُخَافِتْ بِهَا وَابْتَغِ بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ سَبِيلًا
versets.tr: quli dʿū llāha awi dʿū r-raḥmāna ayyan mā tadʿū fa-lahu l-asmāʾu l-ḥusnā wa-lā tajhar bi-ṣalātika wa-lā tukhāfit bihā wa-btaghi bayna dhālika sabīlan
versets.trad: Say: “Call upon God, or call upon the All-Merciful; by whichever name you call, His are the most beautiful Names.” And neither raise your voice too loud in your prayer nor lower it too much, but seek a way between that.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the divine Names the garden of the saints: among God's signal favors toward His friends: their strolling, with their secrets, through the orchards of His remembrance, going from garden to garden and from intimacy to intimacy, through the telling of His most beautiful Names; the rich repose in their orchards, the friends stroll in the places of their glorification, breathing what gleams to their secrets of the unveilings of His majesty and beauty. And the middle way of the voice: neither aloud, that the enemies hear it, nor so low that the friends hear it not.

mots.racine: د ع و / ر ح م
mots.sens: call upon Allāh or the All-Merciful
mots.def: oneness of the Named under all Names.

mots.racine: س م و / ح س ن
ب غ ي / س ب ل

versets.ar: وَقُلِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي لَمْ يَتَّخِذْ وَلَدًا وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ شَرِيكٌ فِي الْمُلْكِ وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ وَلِيٌّ مِنَ الذُّلِّ ۖ وَكَبِّرْهُ تَكْبِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-quli l-ḥamdu li-llāhi lladhī lam yattakhidh waladan wa-lam yakun lahu sharīkun fi l-mulki wa-lam yakun lahu waliyyun mina dh-dhulli wa-kabbirhu takbīran
versets.trad: And say: “Praise be to God, who has taken no son, who has no partner in the dominion, and who needs no protector from humiliation.” And magnify Him with all magnification!
versets.commentaire: The sūra seals on the praise of pure transcendence: God without son, without partner, without a protector drawn from any humiliation. Qushayrī gives two senses to “no protector from humiliation”: either that He has never been abased so as to need a protector, or that He has taken none as ally on account of some baseness to be warded off by that alliance. And on “magnify Him”: know that you reach Him only through Him, not through your own magnifying — even the praise is a gift.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / و ل د
و ل ي / ذ ل ل
